results.title: Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance
results.citation: Guowei Xia et al. (2026) Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance doi:10.1002/adfm.76312
results.abstract: In recent years, fiber‐reinforced epoxy resin‐based composites have become commonly used for insulating components in high‐voltage transmission and transformation equipment. The high withstand voltage performance of materials is crucial to ensuring the safe and stable operation of equipment and the power grid. Therefore, in this paper, a multi‐layer charge regulation engineering was designed on the aramid fiber's surface to regulate the distribution and transmission of charges, thereby achieving an efficient enhancement of the withstand voltage of the composites. This engineering utilizes the charge trapping characteristics of insulating materials and the charge transport properties of semiconductor materials. The effectiveness of this engineering was validated through the tests of flashover voltage and breakdown strength. After constructing the optimal multi‐layer charge regulation structure, the flashover voltage increased by 62.03%, and the breakdown strength improved by 65.22%. Combining charge dissipation and trap distribution, interface bond strength, molecular dynamics simulations, and phase‐field modeling results reveals the mechanism of the multi‐layer charge regulation engineering. This can be summarized as the charge trapping in the F‐Group layer, uniform charge distribution in the ZnO nanowire layer, and deep charge transport in the FePc layer, which together ensure the effective enhancement of the material's withstand voltage performance.

results.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals
results.citation: Tongbo He et al. (2025) Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals doi:10.1109/ICEEPS66790.2025.11239566
results.venue: 2025 4th International Conference on Energy and Electrical Power Systems (ICEEPS)
results.abstract: As core equipment for grid condition monitoring, power transmission and transformation sensing terminals face critical challenges in ultra-high altitude extreme environments, including material performance degradation, abnormal electrical characteristics, and inadequate operational adaptability. Existing environmental testing standards demonstrate limitations in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation capabilities and outdated control strategies. This paper proposes an ultra-high altitude adaptability testing methodology for power transmission sensing terminals based on multidimensional environmental coupling loading and dynamic gradient control. Through retrofitting a comprehensive test chamber with integrated ultraviolet irradiation, air circulation, and low-pressure control modules, we established a synchronized multi-environmental factor loading system. The implementation of a pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm coupled with a dynamic gradient control model enables precise simulation of altitude gradient variations. Experimental results demonstrate the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s), while significantly reducing testing cycle duration. This research provides technical support for reliability evaluation of power transmission equipment in ultra-high altitude regions such as the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, demonstrating substantial engineering application value.

results.title: Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering
results.citation: Xiaolong Wang et al. (2025) Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering doi:10.1109/ICPICS66386.2025.11347183
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Power, Intelligent Computing and Systems (ICPICS)
results.abstract: In order to improve the flexibility and safety of power supply of electrical engineering systems in scenarios such as high-voltage isolation, complex environments and mobile devices, this paper studies wireless power transmission technology and its application in smart substations, comprehensively and systematically sorts out three types of wireless power supply mechanisms: electromagnetic induction, magnetic resonance coupling and microwave transmission, builds a wireless power supply system framework with magnetic resonance coupling as the core, and deploys the system in 110 kV smart substation scenarios. Test results show that the system achieves an average transmission efficiency of 78.6 % at a coupling distance of 2.8 meters, controls the power fluctuation rate within plus or minus 2.6 %, and reduces the annual interruption frequency to 3 times. The comprehensive operation stability and safety far exceed the traditional wired method. Wireless power transmission shows good engineering practice feasibility and expansion potential, and is suitable for key parts in smart grids that have high requirements for flexibility, safety and efficiency of energy supply.

results.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission
results.citation: Yaru Zhang et al. (2018) System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission doi:10.1063/1.5013177
results.venue: Journal of Renewable and Sustainable Energy
results.abstract: Energy transportation is an important issue for China's electricity system due to the unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands. The traditional method of energy transportation (e.g., coal transportation) is challenging due to both the long distance of transportation and the large amount of energy that it requires. In recent years, China has been developing ultra-high voltage (UHV) as an alternative way of transmitting energy over a long distance, and many UHV lines are being planned for construction during the 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) period. This paper develops a system optimization model for exploring the optimal pathway for long-distance energy transportation in China during the 13th FYP period from the perspective of the minimum cost of an energy transportation system. The results show that (a) although UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted in the 13th FYP period, coal transportation will still play an important role; (b) eight UHV lines are suggested to be built and put into use as a priority in the 13th FYP period; (c) the high technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants would promote the adoption of UHV. Based on these results, some policy suggestions are provided.Energy transportation is an important issue for China's electricity system due to the unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands. results.title: Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems
results.citation: Rab Nawaz et al. (2026) Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems doi:10.1038/s41598-026-49195-7
results.abstract: Modern power systems incorporate ultra-high-voltage transmission lines with series compensation to enhance their reliability and stability. These configurations generate complex data patterns in electrical signals, which present challenges for accurate fault diagnosis. This complexity arises from various potential faults, such as high-impedance faults and inductive-capacitive interactions, compounded by data scarcity owing to privacy and cybersecurity constraints. This study employs a simulation-based analysis to facilitate fault detection, classification, and localization as unified Multi-Classification tasks. The methodology improves the time-frequency domain data through meta-feature engineering and utilizes consensus-based feature selection by combining gradient boosting, variance thresholding, and mutual information. Model learning is conducted using Bayesian optimization within a Stacking Ensemble, integrating Extra Trees, Light Gradient Boosting Machine, and Extreme Gradient Boosting with an optimized Random Forest meta-classifier. The evaluation considers the accuracy, computational time, inference speed, and memory usage under challenging conditions to address practical limitations. The results indicate that the Stacking Ensemble achieves the highest performance despite its greater computational demand. Extra Trees offers the fastest execution but shows decreased accuracy with increased classification complexity. The Light Gradient Boosting Machine and Extreme Gradient Boosting demonstrate optimized performance across tasks owing to enhanced data processing. These findings confirm the practical applicability of the proposed fault diagnosis framework to modern ultra-high-voltage power systems.

results.title: Particle Swarm Optimization with Stagnation Counter Technique for Optimization of Main Installation Materials Cost in Power Transmission and Transformation Projects
results.citation: Yuliang Jiang et al. (2026) Particle Swarm Optimization with Stagnation Counter Technique for Optimization of Main Installation Materials Cost in Power Transmission and Transformation Projects doi:10.1109/ICICACS68679.2026.11578464
results.venue: 2026 4th International Conference on Integrated Circuits and Communication Systems (ICICACS)
results.abstract: The cost-effective power transmission is the bulk movement of electrical energy form generating stations to substations located near demand centers. The long distance using high voltage lines and it serves as the critical bridge in the electrical grid, ensuring that energy produced as efficient, remote location reaches homes and industries. The conductors, insulators, towers and transformers contribute major portion of the total investment in power transmission and transformation projects. Selection of suitable main installation materials configurations is crucial for minimizing project costs while maintaining high transmission efficiency and system reliability. The existing optimization algorithms focus on overall project investment and faced the inefficient energy transmission problem, when the existing algorithm are unable to concentrate the multiple crucial aspects simultaneously and stuck into the local optima. To mitigate this problem, the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm with Stagnation Counter Technique (SCT) is used proposed. The PSO is used to concentrate the multiple aspects simultaneously based on the iterative search spaces utilizing swarm collective experience recursively. The SCT is used to overcome the premature convergence problem which is occurred by the PSO, by forcing the algorithm to resume the exploration when the progress is stopped. The proposed PSO-SCT framework is achieved better results comparing to some models such as Standard Particle Swarm Optimization (SPSO), Fully Informed Particle Swarm Optimization (FIPSO). Thus, the proposed PSO-SCT based approach optimizes conductor type, conductor cross-sectional area, transformer capacity, tower configuration, and insulation selection to reduce main installation materials cost while satisfying transmission-efficiency and reliability constraints.

Thus, the proposed PSO-SCT based approach optimizes conductor type, conductor cross-sectional area, transformer capacity, tower configuration, and insulation selection to reduce main installation materials cost while satisfying transmission-efficiency and reliability constraints.

results.title: Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer
results.citation: Naveen Garg et al. (2025) Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer doi:10.1109/CISES66934.2025.11264962
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent and Secure Engineering Solutions (CISES)
results.abstract: The direct transmission systems using the voltage of one of the largest values, HVDC, that allow transmitting electricity with high efficiency and safety over long distances may be considered one of the most important milestones in the history of electricity engineering and development of the electrical power systems. It has a few strengths such as; it has a lower power loss compared with standard alternating current (AC) systems, is more stable on the grid, and has power density. The fundamental units of the HVDC technology are transformer stations, lines and control systems. HVDC converters technology has made the major focus on voltage source converters (VSCs) and line-commutated converters (LCCs) in power conversion between AC and DC to achieve maximum efficiency of the system. Renewable energy: Here HVDC can adopt renewable energy sources like wind power and solar energy to ease transfer of power through modular and long-distance power transmission of remote generation sites to the centre of consumption. Technology HVDC is being developed in the direction of higher converter efficiency, reduced cost and decreased fault tolerance. Enhanced control algorithms and real-time monitoring software guarantee dynamic stability and easy interface with current grid infrastructures. With the information on the cleaner energy future, an even further progress of the HVDC systems that will be incorporated in the world energy transition on the way to more sustainable and resilient electricity power systems is proceeding.

results.title: Application of Integrated Optical Electric-Field Sensor on the Measurements of Transient Voltages in AC High-Voltage Power Grids
results.citation: S. Xie et al. (2019) Application of Integrated Optical Electric-Field Sensor on the Measurements of Transient Voltages in AC High-Voltage Power Grids doi:10.3390/APP9091951
results.abstract: Transient voltages in the power grid are the key for the fault analysis of a power grid, optimized insulation design, and the standardization of the high-voltage testing method. The traditional measuring equipment, based on electrical engineering, normally has a limited bandwidth and response speed, which are also featured by a huge size and heavy weight. In this paper, an integrated optical electric-field sensor based on the Pockels effect was developed and applied to measure the transient voltages on the high-voltage conductors in a non-contact measuring mode. The measuring system has a response speed faster than 6 ns and a wide bandwidth ranging from 5 Hz to 100 MHz. Moreover, the sensors have the dimensions of 18 mm by 18 mm by 48 mm and a light weight of dozens of grams. The measuring systems were employed to monitor the lightning transient voltages on a 220 kV overhead transmission line. The switching transient voltages were also measured by the measuring system during the commissioning of the 500 kV middle Tibet power grid. In 2017, 307 lightning transient voltages caused by induction stroke were recorded. The characteristics of these voltage waveforms are different from the standard lightning impulse voltage proposed by IEC standards. Three types of typical switching transient voltage in 500 kV AC power grid were measured, and the peak values of these overvoltages can reach 1.73 times rated voltage.

results.title: Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering
results.citation: D. Danylchenko et al. (2025) Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering doi:10.20998/eree.2025.1(10).333342
results.venue: Bulletin of the National Technical University "KhPI". Series: Energy: Reliability and Energy Efficiency
results.abstract: The article presents a comprehensive study of China's energy security from a technical and economic perspective, taking into account current challenges and strategic goals of the energy transition. The main focus is on the quantitative analysis of structural problems that threaten the stability of the energy system. In particular, based on 2023 data, it was found that the structure of energy consumption remains dependent on fossil fuels: the share of coal is 56.4 %, and oil import dependence is 72.6 %. In addition, the probability of overloading the power grid in extreme weather conditions was found to be 7.3 %, indicating the system's vulnerability to climate change. The study modeled the effectiveness of the implementation of Smart Grid technologies, which increased the efficiency of ultra-high voltage power transmission by 18 %. The study also recorded a 40 % increase in the total capacity of virtual power plants and an increase in the internal rate of return of energy storage systems based on lithium batteries to 8.7 %. From the perspective of long-term strategic planning, the proposed multi-energy flow model estimates the cost of transporting 500 km of green hydrogen at 35 yuan/kg, and also forms a standardized power compensation mechanism (0.08 yuan/kW). The empirical results confirm the feasibility of such measures: the rate of rejection of new transmission lines in the northwestern regions has been reduced to 3.2 %, and the introduction of 12 GW of energy storage systems in the Yangtze Delta has reduced peak load by 15%. It is also demonstrated that the cost of hydrogen production from offshore wind energy has reached 0.3 yuan/kWh, or 35 yuan/kg, taking into account market electricity tariffs. The article proposes strategic measures to ensure energy security, including investments in scientific and technological development in the amount of 0.8 % of gross domestic product, modernization of the power grid with funding of 4.2 trillion yuan over the next 10 years, and the creation of an adaptive political monitoring system. These results form the basis for a systematic electrical engineering approach to ensuring China's energy sustainability in the context of global transformations.

results.title: Comparing a Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current and Unified Power Flow Control for Short Circuit Level Reduction in Iraqi 400 Kv Grid
results.citation: A. N. Merzah et al. (2024) Comparing a Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current and Unified Power Flow Control for Short Circuit Level Reduction in Iraqi 400 Kv Grid doi:10.51244/ijrsi.2024.1107018
results.venue: International journal of research and scientific innovation
results.abstract: The demand for electricity has increased significantly in recent years; yet, the transmission and distribution networks are unable to meet unrestrained demands because of resource constraint. Losses in power lines exacerbate the unfavorable conditions for maximum power transfer. Recent decades have seen a rise in the use of FACTS and High Voltage Direct Current transmission in power systems due to its many advantages, which include reduced short circuit level (SCL), enhanced voltage profiles, reduced network power losses, and enhanced system reliability and safety. The paper presents a comparing study of the effectiveness of Unified Power Flow Control (UPFC) and Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current (MT-HVDC) transmission in reducing short circuit current and network power losses for the Iraqi Super High Voltage (ISHV) grid. Modeling of Unified Power Flow Control and Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current transmission is carried out using the Power System Simulator for Engineering (PSS/E) version 32 Package Program. The integration of multi-terminal HVDC transmission to the system resulted in a considerable decrease in short circuit levels and network power losses for the majority of buses in the system, as well as a reduction in the loading on the transmission lines in the southern region of Iraqi Super High Voltage grid, As a result, the South’s network performance will improve, hence improving Iraq’s electrical power system. Furthermore, when compared to installing a UPFC, the results showed that multi-terminal HVDC transmission reduces short circuit levels more efficiently.

results.title: Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale
results.citation: Ping Xiong et al. (2025) Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11088518
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
results.abstract: The rapid integration of variable renewable energy into county-level power grids has introduced significant operational challenges, particularly transmission congestion and transformer overload caused by bi-directional flow. To overcome the restriction of conventional grid reinforcement solutions focused mainly on infrastructure expansion, the work develops an integrated generation-grid-load-storage coordination framework adapted to scenarios with high penetration of renewables. The solution achieves localized energy balance through optimized energy storage allocation and operational modes control, establishing a resilient power grid architecture capable of grid-connected interaction and autonomous islanded operation. Renovation schemes of building a power system with ultra-high renewable penetration ratios are presented, in which technical routes for the coordination framework are investigated. Performance of key technologies, including grid-forming voltage source converters, smooth mode transition, and the collective control of the coordination system in support of the independent operation and stability of a 100% variable inverter-based renewable energy sources power system, is validated.

results.title: Ultra-High Voltage NV Center Magnetic Sensing System Based on Power over Fiber
results.citation: Linfeng Zhan et al. (2025) Ultra-High Voltage NV Center Magnetic Sensing System Based on Power over Fiber doi:10.3390/photonics12111093
results.abstract: Aiming to address the insulation and power supply challenges faced by electrical measurement in ultra-high voltage (UHV) environments, this study proposes and implements a nitrogen-vacancy (NV) center magnetic sensing system based on Power over Fiber (PoF) technology. The system adopts a high-voltage and low-voltage separation design, realizing the isolated transmission of electrical energy and the reliable recovery of measurement signals through an optical fiber link. The sensing unit on the high-voltage side is composed of NV center sensors, microwave excitation modules, and signal processing modules. Its power supply is provided by an independently developed high-power laser power converter (LPC) assembly via 830 nm optical fiber laser transmission. Under an optical input of 10 W, this assembly can achieve an electrical output of 4.88 W with a conversion efficiency of 48.9%. The experimental results show that the system can operate stably in a simulated UHV environment; by optimizing modulation parameters, the optimal magnetic measurement sensitivity reaches 6.1 nT/Hz1/2. This research provides a safe and reliable solution for the power supply and precise sensing of high-potential side equipment in UHV scenarios, and demonstrates the application potential of PoF technology in advanced sensing for power systems.

results.title: NV centers magnetic sensing system in ultra-high-voltage utilizing power over fiber
results.citation: Xiaofei Li et al. (2026) NV centers magnetic sensing system in ultra-high-voltage utilizing power over fiber doi:10.1088/1742-6596/3174/1/012058
results.abstract: This paper proposes and experimentally studies a magnetic sensing system that employs nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond and utilizes power over fiber (PoF) technology for ultra-high voltage (UHV) scenarios. The structure and working principle of the system are introduced. The designed PoF link delivers a maximum power output of 3.8 W with an electrical transmission efficiency of 38%, while the designed sensor exhibits outstanding electromagnetic stability within the 2.867 GHz frequency band. Finally, the system ultimately attains a sensitivity of 6.1 nT/Hz1/2 for magnetic measurements.

results.title: Study on Ecological Environmental Impact Assessment Index System of UHV AC Transmission and Transformation Engineering-A Case Study of 1000kv UHV Transmission and Transformation Engineering from Zhangbei to Xiongan
results.citation: Jiming Song et al. (2023) Study on Ecological Environmental Impact Assessment Index System of UHV AC Transmission and Transformation Engineering-A Case Study of 1000kv UHV Transmission and Transformation Engineering from Zhangbei to Xiongan doi:10.1051/e3sconf/202337002001
results.abstract: Ultra-high voltage (UHV) power grid is a new transmission and transformation technology, which has the advantages of large transmission capacity, long transmission distance, low line loss, saving land resources and obvious economic benefits. Taking the Zhangbei-xiongan 1000kv AC high voltage power transmission and transformation project as an example, this paper constructs the eco-environmental index system of the UHV transmission and transformation project by using the pressure-state-response model, and constructs a set of 18 indexes eco-environmental index system, it mainly includes three aspects: the eco-environmental pressure index caused by the construction behavior in different construction stages of power transmission and transformation projects, and the eco-environmental state index given by on-the-spot investigation and study of projects, based on the construction unit on the ecological environment impact management response measures given indicators. The index system provides a basis for comprehensive and systematic evaluation of the impact of power transmission and distribution projects on the ecological environment, and finally draws the corresponding project construction evaluation conclusions. In this paper, the eco-environmental impact assessment index system of power transmission and transformation engineering has certain universality, and it has certain reference and guiding significance for the ecological environment evaluation of power transmission and transformation engineering.

papergraph_input.abstract: Ultra-high voltage (UHV) power grid is a new transmission and transformation technology, which has the advantages of large transmission capacity, long transmission distance, low line loss, saving land resources and obvious economic benefits. Taking the Zhangbei-xiongan 1000kv AC high voltage power transmission and transformation project as an example, this paper constructs the eco-environmental index system of the UHV transmission and transformation project by using the pressure-state-response model, and constructs a set of 18 indexes eco-environmental index system, it mainly includes three aspects: the eco-environmental pressure index caused by the construction behavior in different construction stages of power transmission and transformation projects, and the eco-environmental state index given by on-the-spot investigation and study of projects, based on the construction unit on the ecological environment impact management response measures given indicators. The index system provides a basis for comprehensive and systematic evaluation of the impact of power transmission and distribution projects on the ecological environment, and finally draws the corresponding project construction evaluation conclusions. In this paper, the eco-environmental impact assessment index system of power transmission and transformation engineering has certain universality, and it has certain reference and guiding significance for the ecological environment evaluation of power transmission and transformation engineering.

results.title: Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid
results.citation: Zheng Bowen et al. (2018) Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid doi:10.1109/CICED.2018.8592243
results.venue: China International Conference on Electricity Distribution
results.abstract: With the continuous operation of ultra-high voltage (UHV) AC and DC power transmission and transformation projects in Inner Mongolia autonomous region, Inner Mongolia power grid has become a UHV AC and DC hybrid system. However, the development of AC and DC projects is not balanced, and the characteristics of “strong DC and weak AC” are obvious, It is bound to affect the safe and stable operation of UHV power grids, when sending end matching thermal power units connecting to UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, the reliability of power supply is difficult to be guaranteed. Taking the sending end regional power grid of Xilinhot to Taizhou (Jiangsu Province) UHV DC transmission project (hereinafter referred to as Xitai DC) as the research object, by using Power System Analysis Software Package simulation software, this paper deeply analyzes the influence of high-power matching power supply on the safe and stable operation of the power grid after connecting to the UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, and put forward the corresponding prevention and control measures.

results.title: A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables
results.citation: Zifan Zhang et al. (2025) A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables doi:10.1109/ICLCSE66889.2025.11389670
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
results.abstract: In the context of accelerating urbanisation and the transformation of energy structures, high voltage power cables have emerged as a pivotal transmission method within contemporary power systems. The operational reliability of these entities has been demonstrated to have a direct impact on grid security and power supply quality. Within cable structures, the metallic shielding layer fulfils a dual function: it both suppresses electromagnetic interference and protects communication facilities, while also providing a safe return path for ground faults and preventing electric shock accidents. The present study investigates the mechanisms and characteristics of short-circuit faults in cable shields. Utilising the electromagnetic transient simulation software PSCAD, a refined cable model incorporating frequency-dependent parameters was constructed. The electrical characteristics and influencing factors of such faults were revealed through analysis of the variation patterns of shield currents and zero-sequence currents under different operating conditions. The present research provides both theoretical and empirical support for the rapid diagnosis and condition assessment in engineering applications.

results.title: Analysis of the Influence of UHV AC Access on Power Transmission and Receiving Capability of Provincial Power Grid
results.citation: Xiaofan Su et al. (2024) Analysis of the Influence of UHV AC Access on Power Transmission and Receiving Capability of Provincial Power Grid doi:10.1109/iciba62489.2024.10867916
results.venue: 2024 IEEE 4th International Conference on Information Technology, Big Data and Artificial Intelligence (ICIBA)
results.abstract: With the increasing scale of UHV DC infeed and the large-scale development of renewable energy in the province, the AC-DC hybrid characteristics of the power grid are more complex. After the ultra-high voltage DC bipolar blocking and transferred to the inter-regional connecting line, the power shortage is reduced, and the coupling characteristics between the 500kV regional interconnection line are weakened. The maximum inter-regional power flow and the receiving power of the ultra-high voltage DC are increased after the ultra-high voltage AC network is built. The existing problems of the actual provincial power grid are analyzed. Then, based on the UHV AC drop point, the power flow change of the main 500kV transmission line in the provincial power grid is analyzed under the access system scheme and different operation modes. Finally, from the aspect of improving the overall external power-receiving capability of the power grid, the improvement level of the power-receiving capability of the provincial power grid after UHV AC operation under different schemes is compared and analyzed.

results.title: Active Power Coordinated Control of Grid-Forming Renewable Generations and DC Transmission System
results.citation: Zhi Jing et al. (2024) Active Power Coordinated Control of Grid-Forming Renewable Generations and DC Transmission System doi:10.1109/ICPEE64457.2024.10950001
results.venue: 2024 8th International Conference on Power and Energy Engineering (ICPEE)
results.abstract: In recent years, the large-scale wind and photovoltaic power bases have been actively promoted in desert and arid regions, with the construction of ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission channels. However, faults in the DC transmission channels of these new energy bases can lead to short-term active power fluctuations, potentially causing instability in the angle and frequency deviations in the sending-end power system. This paper first establishes a model of a wind-solar power base under ideal conditions, where the wind power system employs a network-forming control strategy to enhance grid connection stability, while the photovoltaic system continues to use the traditional grid-following control. Through theoretical analysis of the spatiotemporal distribution characteristics of frequency in the new energy power system, the paper explores the factors influencing these distribution characteristics, discusses the necessity of studying these characteristics, and develops a coordinated energy flow control strategy for the wind-solar power base. Simulation results verify the accuracy of the proposed frequency indices and the effectiveness of the control strategy, summarizing the frequency variation patterns considering spatiotemporal distribution effects.

results.title: Comparative assessment of fault detection methods in transmission line during power swing: analyzing single and hybrid networks for the southern Kerala grid
results.citation: S. K et al. (2024) Comparative assessment of fault detection methods in transmission line during power swing: analyzing single and hybrid networks for the southern Kerala grid doi:10.1088/2631-8695/ad3895
results.abstract: The blocking function of power oscillation is a highly effective feature found in distance relays. Its purpose is to prevent unintentional tripping of transmission lines. It is crucial to accurately differentiate between faults and power oscillations to minimise the occurrence of widespread power outages and monetary losses. Therefore, the detection and classification of faults arising from power swings are critical challenges in ensuring the smooth operation and overall health of transmission lines, particularly in extra-high-voltage (EHV) and ultra-high-voltage (UHV) power systems. Modern methods rely on machine learning methods which are in their infancy. The paper proposes to identify various fault conditions in transmission lines during power swing by using a hybrid Neural Network architecture. In this research, different designs such as 2-D convolution neural network (2D-CNN), 1-D convolution neural network (1D-CNN), long short-term memory (LSTM), and CNN-LSTM hybrid networks are investigated to understand the effect of various deep learning approaches. Along with the southern Kerala grid, the standard 9-bus system is selected as a test case to examine various fault circumstances of the system during power swing. Multiple tests were conducted to determine the optimal deep learning architecture, including model parameters and configurations, for accurate fault detection. For the 9 bus system, the 1D CNN network performs better with an accuracy of 98.70%, and for the Kerala grid, both networks are competitive, but the CNN-LSTM Hybrid method slightly outperforms the 1D CNN with an accuracy of 92.40%.

results.title: Geomagnetically Induced Current Calculation of High Voltage Power System With Long Transmission Lines Using Kriging Method
results.citation: Yu-hao Chen et al. (2022) Geomagnetically Induced Current Calculation of High Voltage Power System With Long Transmission Lines Using Kriging Method doi:10.1109/TPWRD.2021.3068216
results.abstract: Calculation of geomagnetically induced current (GIC) flowing through power system during the geomagnetic storm has attracted more attention recently. However, for high voltage power systems with transmission lines over hundreds or even thousands of kilometers, the earth model and geomagnetical field generally vary significantly. So, it is essential to take them into consideration using limited earth survey sites and geomagnetic observatories. To address this problem, a Kriging method is introduced in this paper to make earth model and geomagnetical field interpolations. It has the characteristic of spatial autocorrelation by considering not only the distances between predicted points and training points but also the distances between training points themselves. Finally, a case study of the Central China 1000 kV ultra-high voltage (UHV) grid is carried out to illustrate the applicability and effectiveness of the proposed method.

results.title: Development and Experimental Study of De-Icing Short-Circuit Device for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines Based on Rapid Clamping Structure
results.citation: Jinpei Wang et al. (2026) Development and Experimental Study of De-Icing Short-Circuit Device for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines Based on Rapid Clamping Structure doi:10.1109/ICSGGE69348.2026.11508193
results.venue: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
results.abstract: To address the issues of inconvenient installation and poor adaptability associated with traditional fixed crimping drainage lines during sectionalized direct-current (DC) icemelting of transmission lines, this paper aims to develop a novel phase-to-phase short-circuit device for conductor ice-melting. The device adopts a “T-shaped” aluminum alloy body and a modular quick-press cover plate. It significantly enhances heat dissipation performance by utilizing the reserved air convection gaps. Through a highly reliable hinge and a quick-pressing mechanism, it achieves non-pressurized quick assembly and disassembly and stable connection with the wires. Experimental results demonstrate that under the rated current required by engineering standards, the steady-state temperature rise of the device is lower than that of the conductor. Furthermore, the ratio of its DC resistance to that of an equal-length conductor is less than 1.1, satisfying standard requirements. Characterized by its lightweight structure, convenient installation, and reliable current-carrying capability, the device flexibly adapts to various bundle conductors and two-phase/three-phase short-circuiting scenarios. It significantly enhances the efficiency and safety of emergency icemelting operations, providing effective technical equipment for the power grid to mitigate icing hazards.

results.title: The Seismic Response of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines
results.citation: Wei Lv et al. (2023) The Seismic Response of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines doi:10.1088/1742-6596/2557/1/012065
results.abstract: As an important part of the substation, the seismic capacity of ultra-high voltage (UHV) electrical equipment is related to the safe and stable operation of the substation and the whole power grid. The UHV gas-insulated transmission lines (GIL) have many structural characteristics, such as long pipeline distance, the large span between supports and the obvious amplification effect of supports. Its natural frequency is close to the predominant frequency of the seismic wave, resulting in its susceptibility to resonance or resonance-like and high seismic susceptibility. In order to analyze the seismic performance of UHV GIL better, the vibration mode decomposition response spectrum method and the dynamic time-history analysis method are used to analyze the seismic response of UHV GIL in this paper. The search for seismic weak positions of GIL is a reference for effectively improving the seismic performance of UHV GIL.

results.title: THE DISTRIBUTIONAL EFFECT OF INTER-REGIONAL TRANSMISSION GRID EXPANSION ON CHINA’S POWER INDUSTRY
results.citation: XU Tan et al. (2022) THE DISTRIBUTIONAL EFFECT OF INTER-REGIONAL TRANSMISSION GRID EXPANSION ON CHINA’S POWER INDUSTRY doi:10.1142/s201000782240005x
results.abstract: China suffers significant heterogeneities in resource endowments and geological conditions across the regions. Ultra-high voltage (UHV) line construction is a project put forward by the government to boost the inter-regional transmission grid expansion, benefiting economic development by reducing pollutant emissions and absorbing more clean energy. This study investigates the extent to which this giant infrastructure has distributional effects on China’s power industry. We estimate the distributional effect of UHV lines from the perspective of power deployment, carbon emissions, and producer surplus by simulating an equilibrium model of the national electricity market through the mixed complementarity problems (MCPs) method. Furthermore, the Lorenz curve is also employed to investigate the distributional effect of UHV introduction. The results indicate that: (1) the distributional effect of introducing UHV lines is regressive considering its limited contributions to the equalization of environmental and economic welfare; (2) the emission reduction effect of the UHV lines has been improved between 2015 and 2018; (3) power plants with a large installation capacity could seek more economic profits while emitting more pollution; (4) the deployment of clean power in the resource-abundant regions is far below its potential. Meanwhile, we propose improving the structure and technology of the power mix, which can accelerate China’s market-oriented reform of the power system by equally distributing the benefits.

results.title: 500kV Ultra-High Voltage Cable Fine Manufacturing Technology Research
results.citation: Tao Zhang et al. (2025) 500kV Ultra-High Voltage Cable Fine Manufacturing Technology Research doi:10.1109/ICEMPE66159.2025.11123085
results.venue: International Conference on Electrical Materials and Power Equipment
results.abstract: Guangzhou Chuting (Suixi) power transmission and transformation project is the first 500kV ultra-high voltage urban power grid (cable) project of China Southern Power Grid, of which $500 \text{kV} 2500 ~\text{mm}^{2}$ ultra-high voltage cable is the longest transmission distance, the highest voltage level and the largest capacity of the onshore ultra-high voltage cable system in China, and it is also the power cable with the highest voltage level of international cross-linked cable. With years of experience in ultra-high voltage cable manufacturing, Qingdao Hanhe Cable Co., Ltd. has refined the connection of key core processes such as conductor stranding, insulation extrusion, wrinkled aluminum sleeve, hot melt adhesive spraying, etc., and made full use of digitalization, intelligence, informatization and other management methods to successfully complete the project product manufacturing. The completion of this project reflects the ability of localized 500kV ultra-high voltage cable, which is of great significance to promoting the high-end development of China's cable project.

results.title: Transformative AI Technologies in High-Voltage Systems: A Review of Advances in Predictive Maintenance, Fault Detection, and Grid Optimization
results.citation: Ian B. Benitez et al. (2025) Transformative AI Technologies in High-Voltage Systems: A Review of Advances in Predictive Maintenance, Fault Detection, and Grid Optimization doi:10.1109/IICAIET67254.2025.11265217
results.venue: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Engineering and Technology
results.abstract: High voltage engineering has evolved rapidly, driven by the growing need for efficient energy transmission and the integration of renewable energy into modern power grids, including urban areas. Innovations such as HVDC systems are central to this transformation, ensuring that grids can handle the increasing complexity and demand for sustainable energy. However, challenges remain, especially when it comes to coordinating insulation in hybrid AC/DC systems and maintaining the resilience of the overall infrastructure. This review looks at how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can help tackle these challenges, focusing on its role in fault detection, predictive maintenance, and improving system reliability. By comparing traditional methods with AI-driven solutions, we highlight how AI can enhance the scalability, efficiency, and adaptability of power systems. With AI, utilities can predict and prevent faults, optimize grid performance, and seamlessly integrate renewable energy sources into both rural and urban environments. Our goal is to provide insights for researchers, industry professionals, and policymakers on how AI can be harnessed to build more sustainable, resilient, and reliable energy systems. The insights shared here aim to help shape the future of power grids, positioning AI as a key player in the transition to cleaner, more efficient energy solutions.

results.title: Knowledge reasoning in power grid infrastructure projects based on deep multi-view graph convolutional network
results.citation: Jie Hu et al. (2024) Knowledge reasoning in power grid infrastructure projects based on deep multi-view graph convolutional network doi:10.3389/fenrg.2023.1339416
results.abstract: With the rapid development of power grid infrastructure, especially the increasing number of ultra-high voltage (UHV) projects, knowledge extracted from historical engineering data is collected and can be potentially used to assist in the review of power transmission and transformation projects. However, conventional knowledge modeling and knowledge reasoning methods cannot meet the current needs of power grid construction. In this paper, considering the more supernumerary and distinctive information brought by multi-view data which could be beneficial for feature representation and knowledge reasoning from the constructed knowledge base, a multi-view graph convolutional network (GCN) based on knowledge graph is proposed to make classification for power grid infrastructure projects. Specifically, several views are constructed based on attribute information of a knowledge graph. In addition, a Haar convolution-based pooling mechanism is employed to capture the structural features represented by a chain of subgraphs. And then an aggregator that combines both attribute and structural information is used to classify UHV projects. Results from both UHV and NCI-1 datasets indicate that our proposed method is more has higher accuracy and generalization ability.

results.title: Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems
results.citation: Lei Fu et al. (2025) Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11089103
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
results.abstract: The revision of China’s national standard GB/T 22384 addresses critical challenges posed by new-type power grids, characterized by large-scale renewable energy integration, ultra-high voltage AC/DC transmission, and cyber-physical system complexities. Aligned with China’s "dual carbon" strategy and the technical demands of New Power Systems, this revision introduces expanded testing scopes, enhanced network security protection requirements, and refined dynamic simulation methodologies. A case study of the Qing-Yu HVDC project demonstrates the effectiveness of closed-loop test based on real-time digital simulation (RTDS) in validating hierarchical security and stability control systems (SSCS), revealing latent defects such as redundant tripping commands during cascading faults. Innovations lie in integrating RTDS for closed-loop validation of multi-scenario strategies, introducing dynamic state synchronization to mitigate coordination failures, and optimizing lifecycle testing management. The revised standard establishes a robust framework for standardized testing, ensuring reliability and adaptability of SSCS in high-renewable, power electronics-dominated grids. From the main aspects of this revision and the results of engineering empirical tests, the testing methods and technical requirements for SSCS should evolve iteratively in sync with the development process and technological advancements of power systems. SSCS plays a crucial role in ensuring the secure and stable operation of power systems, and the standardization work cannot be achieved just in one effort.

results.title: INTEGRATIVE APPROACH TO STUDYING INSTRUMENTATION AND ELECTRICAL MEASUREMENTS IN THE CONTEXT OF MODERN POWER INDUSTRY REQUIREMENTS
results.citation: Dariia Vilchynska et al. (2026) INTEGRATIVE APPROACH TO STUDYING INSTRUMENTATION AND ELECTRICAL MEASUREMENTS IN THE CONTEXT OF MODERN POWER INDUSTRY REQUIREMENTS doi:10.70651/3041-248x/2026.1.05
results.abstract: The article investigates and theoretically substantiates the concept of an integrative approach to teaching metrological disciplines for students of electrical engineering specialties. The relevance of the research is driven by the rapid digital transformation of the energy sector (Smart Grid, IoT solutions), which imposes new requirements on engineering competencies. The paper provides a thorough analysis of employer expectations, identifying a critical need for specialists capable of integrating metrological knowledge into remote monitoring processes and automated diagnostics of high-voltage equipment. The author proposes a methodological model that involves the convergence of the content of educational components “Control and Measuring Instruments with the Basics of Metrology” and “Metrology and Electrical Measurements” into a single, cohesive system. This model is based on systemic and process management approaches, allowing for the structuring of educational material from fundamental laws of metrology to their practical application in power engineering. Special attention is paid to the practical tools for implementing integrative learning. The experience of implementing STEM technologies and virtual laboratories, which provide simulation of complex industrial processes without risks to expensive equipment, is revealed. The application of the case method is described in detail using examples of solving real-world energy problems: from analyzing the metrological characteristics of smart meters to minimizing losses in commercial electricity metering systems. The conclusions emphasize that the proposed model not only optimizes learning time but also fosters comprehensive professional thinking in students, essential for solving complex tasks in the modern energy industry.

results.title: Voltage Time Series Classification Based on Multi-Domain Transformation and Ensemble Learning
results.citation: Shiyu Yang et al. (2025) Voltage Time Series Classification Based on Multi-Domain Transformation and Ensemble Learning doi:10.1109/DSIS67228.2025.11390584
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Systems (DSIS)
results.abstract: With the continuous growth of electrical load and the widespread integration of dynamic loads, regional power grids face increasing challenges related to transmission capacity limits and short-term voltage stability. Although widearea measurement systems provide abundant data for online stability assessment, achieving rapid and accurate classification in large-scale, complex systems remains a significant challenge. Existing approaches for voltage time series classification often struggle to balance real-time responsiveness with high accuracy, especially when identifying the dominant drivers separating transient instability from short-term voltage instability. To address these challenges, we propose a volTage time serIes classificatioN method based on multi-Domain transformation and Ensemble leaRning (TINDER). The method transforms raw data into multiple discriminative feature spaces, including frequency domain, autocorrelation domain, and shapelet domain, and employs a variety of classifiers such as support vector machines, naive Bayes, decision trees, and elastic distance nearest neighbor. A weighted voting scheme is used to fuses the classification predictions. By utilizing only a small set of features at key time points before and after faults, the method effectively balances classification speed and accuracy. Experiments on the IEEE 39-bus system show that TINDER markedly surpasses traditional methods in both classification accuracy and response time, and reliably identifies different types of voltage instability.

results.title: Multi-Modal Feature Fusion of Electrical and Infrared Signatures using Advanced Tree-Ensembles for Robust Transmission-Line Fault Detection
results.citation: Dr. R. Mythili et al. (2025) Multi-Modal Feature Fusion of Electrical and Infrared Signatures using Advanced Tree-Ensembles for Robust Transmission-Line Fault Detection doi:10.1109/ICAAIC64647.2025.11330845
results.venue: 2025 4th International Conference on Applied Artificial Intelligence and Computing (ICAAIC)
results.abstract: Reliable fault detection in high-voltage transmission lines is critical for maintaining power-system stability, yet most current approaches depend on single-source electrical measurements and simulated training data, limiting their robustness under real-world conditions. This work introduces a multi-modal feature-fusion framework that combines synchronized electrical waveforms and infrared (IR) thermographic imagery to overcome those constraints. Field-deployed phasor measurement units (PMUs) capture high-resolution current-voltage signals while co-located thermal cameras provide real-time surface temperature profiles of conductors and insulators. After signal alignment and noise reduction, a dedicated feature-engineering pipeline extracts statistical, spectral, and texture descriptors from both modalities and merges them into a unified representation. Advanced tree-ensemble classifiers—specifically LightGBM and ExtraTrees—are trained to exploit cross-modal correlations for accurate fault detection and type classification. Experiments on a hybrid dataset of laboratory faults and real transmission-corridor measurements demonstrate greater than 99.5 % accuracy and superior resilience to environmental noise compared with electrical-only baselines. The approach eliminates the need for purely synthetic data, supports explainable decision-making through SHAP analysis, and is readily deployable on edge devices for near-instantaneous fault localization and predictive maintenance in modern smart grids Keywords: Multi-modal feature fusion, Transmission-line fault detection, Infrared thermography, Tree-ensemble learning, Smart grid monitoring

results.title: MEASUREMENT AND CONTROL METHODS IN ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING
results.citation: Taisiia Serdenko et al. (2025) MEASUREMENT AND CONTROL METHODS IN ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING doi:10.23939/istcmtm2025.02.012
results.abstract: The article focuses on innovative measurement and control methods in electrical power engineering, specifically addressing challenges of power quality, signal diagnostics, and automation within smart grids. Emphasis is placed on wavelet analysis, smart metering, IoT integration, and automated control systems. These technologies are examined in the context of enhancing the adaptability and efficiency of modern electrical systems in line with Industry 4.0 requirements. Particular emphasis is placed on wavelet analysis, which serves as a universal tool for diagnosing non-stationary electrical signals, assessing power quality, and detecting harmonic distortions. Thanks to its capability for time-frequency localization, wavelet analysis enables effective signal processing and facilitates tasks such as transient process monitoring, voltage flicker analysis, and improving the accuracy of electrical measurements. This methodology opens new prospects for maintaining the stability of energy systems even under the challenging conditions of renewable energy integration. Special attention is given to the analysis of the role of smart technologies in contemporary energy systems. The advantages of Smart Metering systems—which ensure the automatic collection, analysis, and real-time transmission of energy consumption data—are discussed. This enables efficient management of energy resource distribution, reduces energy losses, and enhances transparency in the relationships between consumers and suppliers. The integration of Smart Metering with Internet of Things (IoT) technologies contributes to the creation of adaptive systems capable of responding to changing conditions in real time, thereby ensuring the stability and efficiency of smart grids. The article also explores the prospects of automated control systems that incorporate intelligent data collection devices and adaptive control algorithms. These systems significantly improve monitoring and diagnostics, facilitate the integration of renewable energy sources, and enhance power quality indicators. In particular, the automation of control processes and the implementation of machine learning technologies open new opportunities for forecasting the behavior of energy systems and increasing their resilience. The solutions presented in the study are aimed at creating adaptive, resilient, and high-tech energy systems that meet the modern challenges of Industry 4.0. Through the integration of wavelet analysis, Smart Metering, IoT, and automated control systems, effective management of energy resources, network stability, and the optimization of energy resource usage in the global energy system can be achieved.

results.title: SMART GIC - RESILIENT POWER GRIDS THROUGH GEOPHYSICAL SENSING AND GRAPH-BASED MODELING
results.citation: CHRISTIAN DAVISON DIRISU et al. (2025) SMART GIC - RESILIENT POWER GRIDS THROUGH GEOPHYSICAL SENSING AND GRAPH-BASED MODELING doi:10.70382/tijert.v09i5.010
results.venue: International Journal of Engineering Research and Technology
results.abstract: Geomagnetically induced currents (GICs), which are ground-level quasi-direct currents induced by space-weather phenomena, pose a serious threat to high-voltage power transmission grids, leading to transformer saturation, reactive power loss, and cascading outages during geomagnetic storms. This paper presents a smart, graph-theory-based framework that bridges real-time geophysical sensing with topology-informed grid modeling to protect power networks proactively. We represent the power grid as a graph data structure: substations and transformers are nodes, and transmission lines are edges; we then embed real-time geoelectric field measurements and space-weather forecasts. This feeds into a graph-based GIC algorithm motivated by sparsity principles of sparse-matrix computations and graph neural networks (GNNs), and trained against comparison grids, such as IEEE test systems and China's 500 kV Shandong network. Validation compared performance and accuracy using real storm instances in the UK and Poland. Our method, enhanced with GNN, detects 20% more high-risk grid sections than traditional approaches and enables optimized GIC mitigation hardware placement (e.g., series capacitors, DC-blockers), maintaining computation speed of approximately 18× owing to graph-parallelization. The models also estimate economic trade-offs, enabling cost-efficient investment in grid hardening and operating decisions in real-time during geomagnetic disturbances. This geophysics, electrical engineering, graph theory, and data science-backed cross-disciplinary approach provides an integrated, scalable solution for GIC threat prediction, mitigation, and management. Our findings verify its promise to enhance the resilience of the grid and ensure a secure electric supply in the age of rising space-weather uncertainty.

results.title: Voltage Control Partitioning Method Based on Weighted Fast-Newman for Ultra High Voltage Direct Current Power Grid
results.citation: Tianhua Chen et al. (2024) Voltage Control Partitioning Method Based on Weighted Fast-Newman for Ultra High Voltage Direct Current Power Grid doi:10.1109/IFEEA64237.2024.10878526
results.venue: 2024 11th International Forum on Electrical Engineering and Automation (IFEEA)
results.abstract: With the infeed UHV-DC merges into the power grid, the division of voltage control partitioning is more challenging, which is an important step in the secondary voltage control of power system. In this paper, the infeed UHV-DC node is equivalent to a reactive power supply, the weighted Fast-Newman algorithm is used to merge the reactive power sources through the maximum distance difference degree. The proposed method makes load nodes be divided with voltage reactive sensitivity and the average power factor. Which not only reflects the power grid by the center of hub AC bus control requirements, and also conforms to the principle of power grid control hierarchical control. By using the method the control partition result is more adaptable and the influence of UHV-DC transmission power of the partition is considered as well. The rationality of the proposed method is verified by the simulation results of IEEE39 node system.

results.title: Analysis of the Impact of Transmitter-Side Electrochemical Energy Storage on Recipient-Side Phase Change Failure in Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Transmission Systems
results.citation: Yiheng Ren (2024) Analysis of the Impact of Transmitter-Side Electrochemical Energy Storage on Recipient-Side Phase Change Failure in Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Transmission Systems doi:10.1109/EEBDA60612.2024.10485721
results.venue: 2024 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Big Data and Algorithms (EEBDA)
results.abstract: Phase change failure is one of the common faults in UHV DC transmission system, which leads to voltage fluctuation, current inrush and other phenomena, thus causing serious harm to the safe operation of power grid. Studying the influencing factors of phase change failure is a prerequisite for suppressing phase change failure, and electrochemical energy storage at the sending end can suppress voltage fluctuation, mitigate current impact, improve system inertia, and play a certain buffer and auxiliary role in the case of phase change failure at the receiving end. Therefore, this paper establishes a set of UHV DC transmission system model and explores the influencing factors of phase change failure. Based on this model, the influence mechanism of electrochemical energy storage at the sending end on the phase change failure at the receiving end is investigated from the three aspects of output power size, inductance size and energy storage capacity. The results show that: (1) the larger the output force is, the lesser the degree of phase change failure is. (2) The larger the grounding inductance is, the lower the degree of phase change failure is. (3) The larger the energy storage capacity is, the lower the degree of phase change failure is.

results.title: Data governance method for unmanned aerial vehicle inspection for power grid equipment management
results.citation: Sijia Zheng et al. (2024) Data governance method for unmanned aerial vehicle inspection for power grid equipment management doi:10.1088/1742-6596/3036/1/012003
results.venue: 2024 6th International Conference on Applied Machine Learning (ICAML)
results.abstract: Power inspection is gradually shifting towards digitization and intelligence, which puts higher requirements on the quality of basic data. In the power production process, the generated data primarily originates from equipment status and maintenance activities. The suboptimal quality of data from these aspects leads to inadequate performance in analytical applications. To address this issue, this paper proposes a UAV inspection data governance method for power grid equipment management. The method involves acquiring UAV inspection data, including basic records and line video data of ultra-high voltage transmission lines, extracting attribute features of equipment from the videos, and comparing them with the equipment records. A data governance approach based on feature engineering is designed, incorporating data verification algorithms for equipment attribute completeness, topological connectivity, nameplate compliance, and measurement consistency. This enables the validation of maintenance data by integrating real-time measurement data. For missing or erroneous information already present in the system, data governance identifies the root causes, supplements missing data, and corrects errors, thereby resolving inconsistencies across information systems. This method provides a solution framework for enhancing the quality of power grid equipment management data and supporting subsequent analytical applications.

results.title: Untangling the Knot of Intellectual Property in Power Engineering: Powering Up Innovation in India
results.citation: Deevanshu Shrivastava (2024) Untangling the Knot of Intellectual Property in Power Engineering: Powering Up Innovation in India doi:10.52783/pst.356
results.abstract: Power engineering technology is a field focused on the practical application of scientific and engineering principles to create, transmit, and utilize electrical power in a safe, reliable, and efficient manner. It encompasses a wide range of areas, including the design, operation, and maintenance of power plants, transmission and distribution grids, and renewable energy systems. Power engineering technicians play a vital role in ensuring the smooth functioning of this complex infrastructure. They work with various equipment, from high-pressure boilers and turbines in power plants to transformers and control systems in electricity grids. Their responsibilities include monitoring system performance, troubleshooting technical issues, implementing maintenance procedures, and adhering to safety regulations. The field is constantly evolving with advancements in renewable energy integration, smart grid technologies, and energy storage solutions. Effective management of intellectual property rights is crucial in this sector to foster innovation and protect these advancements, ultimately contributing to a sustainable and efficient future of power generation and distribution. The power sector is undergoing a rapid transformation, driven by advancements in renewable energy sources, smart grid technologies, and energy storage solutions. These innovations hold immense potential for a more sustainable and efficient energy future. However, intellectual property rights (IPR) play a critical role in fostering innovation and ensuring fair returns for inventors and developers. This research paper analyses the key IPR issues specific to power engineering technology, explores their impact on technological advancement, and proposes potential solutions for a more balanced and effective IPR landscape.

results.title: Research on Control Strategy for Reducing Reactive Power Exchange in ACDC Systems Under Low Power in DC Engineering
results.citation: Wang Ling et al. (2024) Research on Control Strategy for Reducing Reactive Power Exchange in ACDC Systems Under Low Power in DC Engineering doi:10.1109/HVDC62448.2024.10723031
results.abstract: With the large-scale development of new energy, the high proportion of wind and solar renewable energy sources and power electronic devices profoundly affect the dynamic behavior of the power system, further posing challenges to the safe and stable operation of the DC system. Traditional ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) engineering converter stations are equipped with a large number of AC filters. During the switching process of AC filters, there is a dead zone of reactive power in the AC/DC system, and there is always a certain amount of reactive power exchange. According to engineering experience, the dead zone range is about 0.8 times the maximum capacity of a single AC filter group. When the AC filter group is large, the reactive power exchange value between the AC/DC systems also increases. Under the continuous development of new energy in the future, the reactive power support capacity of the communication system may become increasingly limited. At the same time, there are a large number of power electronic devices in the network, and the reactive power injected into the communication system may also pose operational risks to the stability of the communication system. This article mainly proposes a control strategy to reduce the reactive power exchange in the AC/DC system in the case of a large dead zone of reactive power under low power in the Matiari-Lahore DC project. By comparing the reactive power exchange during the lifting process and the number of switching times, the possible applicable solutions and risk points of the current DC transmission system are analyzed, providing feasible solutions for the safe and stable operation of DC transmission projects in the future new power grid.

results.title: Research on Calculation and Influencing Factors Analysis of Temporary Overvoltage at DC Transmission Terminal
results.citation: Libo Zhang et al. (2024) Research on Calculation and Influencing Factors Analysis of Temporary Overvoltage at DC Transmission Terminal doi:10.1109/AIEA62095.2024.10692683
results.venue: 2024 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Electromechanical Automation (AIEA)
results.abstract: In regions where power is generated in China, the proportion of new energy sources being transmitted via ultra-high-voltage direct current (UHVDC) is progressively increasing. Due to the continuous increase in the risk of overvoltage in the sending end system caused by the bipolar blocking of direct current, this poses a detrimental effect on the orderly transmission of new energy sources. Initially, by integrating the typical topology of the sending end’s vicinity of the direct current system, the frequency temporary overvoltage (TOV) of the converter bus after the large-scale integration of new energy sources was quantitatively calculated. Subsequently, by combining the expression for the TOV at the DC converter bus, an analysis on the impact of the new energy integration ratio and the reactive power configuration ratio on the TOV was conducted from both theoretical perspectives and through simulation of typical examples. Lastly, based on practical engineering experience and the foundation of theoretical and example analysis, relevant guidance suggestions are proposed, offering references and guidance for the planning of China’s power grid.

papergraph_input.title: Research on Calculation and Influencing Factors Analysis of Temporary Overvoltage at DC Transmission Terminal
papergraph_input.citation: Libo Zhang et al. (2024) Research on Calculation and Influencing Factors Analysis of Temporary Overvoltage at DC Transmission Terminal doi:10.1109/AIEA62095.2024.10692683
papergraph_input.venue: 2024 5th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Electromechanical Automation (AIEA)
papergraph_input.abstract: In regions where power is generated in China, the proportion of new energy sources being transmitted via ultra-high-voltage direct current (UHVDC) is progressively increasing. Due to the continuous increase in the risk of overvoltage in the sending end system caused by the bipolar blocking of direct current, this poses a detrimental effect on the orderly transmission of new energy sources. Initially, by integrating the typical topology of the sending end’s vicinity of the direct current system, the frequency temporary overvoltage (TOV) of the converter bus after the large-scale integration of new energy sources was quantitatively calculated. Subsequently, by combining the expression for the TOV at the DC converter bus, an analysis on the impact of the new energy integration ratio and the reactive power configuration ratio on the TOV was conducted from both theoretical perspectives and through simulation of typical examples. Lastly, based on practical engineering experience and the foundation of theoretical and example analysis, relevant guidance suggestions are proposed, offering references and guidance for the planning of China’s power grid.

results.title: Has the inter-regional transmission grid promoted clean power development? A quantitative assessment on China’s electricity sector
results.citation: XU Tan et al. (2020) Has the inter-regional transmission grid promoted clean power development? A quantitative assessment on China’s electricity sector doi:10.1016/j.jclepro.2020.122370
results.abstract: Abstract Inter-regional transmission has always been taken as an important measure to better the power mix in China, however, not all the people think it effective in boosting the development of clean power because not all the intensively invested transmission lines are fully occupied. To objectively evaluate the impacts of the introduction of inter-regional transmission lines, especially the ultra-high voltage (UHV) grid, may clarify the disputes and benefit China's electricity policy reform. This work simulates an equilibrium model of China's electricity market through the mixed complementarity problems (MCP) method, to investigate the impacts of the construction of the UHV transmission lines on the deployment of clean power and reduction of carbon emissions under different scenarios. The results indicate that the introduction of the UHV grid has increased the competition in both the power generation and consumption sides, but it has limited contribution to mitigate the curtailment of clean power and carbon emissions, there may exist the hysteresis effect of the UHV grid. We propose the following suggestions to better the development of China's power system. (1) With regard to electricity market coupling, it is more appropriate to firstly connect the regional market in the central and eastern China. (2) Promoting the installed capacity of clean power in a short-run is a necessary auxiliary method to effectively reduce the carbon emissions.

results.title: Overview of Main Electrical Equipment for UHVDC Transmission
results.citation: Xiangyu Meng (2025) Overview of Main Electrical Equipment for UHVDC Transmission doi:10.5220/0014320200004918
results.venue: Proceedings of the 3rd International Conference on Computer Science and Mechatronics
results.abstract: : Ultra-high Voltage Direct Current (UHVDC) transmission is the mainstream solution of modern remote power transmission. Converter from the 12 pulse converter mainly using thyristors to the series capacitor converter, and finally developed to the converter using fully controlled devices. The former uses thyristor devices, and transmission-line system of electric power provide the commutation voltage to complete the commutation, the latter is composed of fully controlled devices, and the commutation is completed by the self-turn-off characteristics of the devices. Transmission line protection is mainly divided into traveling wave protection, differential undervoltage protection and longitudinal differential protection. This study conducts a comparative evaluation and systematic categorization of the principal protection methodologies utilized in transmission line systems, with a focus on delineating their respective merits, limitations, and distinguishing operational characteristics under varying fault scenarios. This paper introduces the converter and transmission protection technology from the aspects of circuit structure, principle, advantages and disadvantages. The final section of this research synthesizes key findings while delineating emerging trajectories for next-generation ultra-high-voltage (UHV) power transmission systems, with particular emphasis on their transformative potential in enabling large-scale renewable energy integration and transcontinental grid interoperability.

results.title: Technical Advancements and Grid Integration Challenges of Modern Wind Power Plants
results.citation: Mr. Sandip Goski and Mr. Ammit Nanaji Akkewar (2026) Technical Advancements and Grid Integration Challenges of Modern Wind Power Plants doi:10.48175/ijarsct-34941
authors: Mr. Sandip Goski and Mr. Ammit Nanaji Akkewar
results.venue: International Journal of Advanced Research in Science, Communication and Technology
results.abstract: Wind energy has emerged as one of the most significant renewable energy resources for achieving sustainable and carbon-neutral electricity generation. The increasing penetration of wind power into modern electrical grids has accelerated advancements in wind turbine technologies, power electronic converters, and grid integration techniques. This paper presents a detailed analysis of modern wind power plant (WPP) technologies, emphasizing the evolution from fixed-speed wind turbines to advanced variable-speed systems. The operational characteristics and performance comparison of Doubly Fed Induction Generator (DFIG) and Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator (PMSG) based wind turbines are discussed comprehensively. Furthermore, major grid integration challenges such as voltage instability, frequency regulation, low-voltage ride-through (LVRT), reactive power support, and harmonic distortion are analyzed. The paper also examines the growing importance of offshore wind farms and High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) transmission systems for large-scale renewable energy integration. Finally, future research directions involving artificial intelligence, smart grids, and energy storage systems are highlighted. The study concludes that advanced power electronics and intelligent control systems are critical for achieving a stable and reliable wind-dominated power system.
