results.title: Three-dimensional electro-thermal coupling analysis of ultra-high-voltage autotransformer based on MPI-PETSc parallel computing framework
results.citation: E. Tianlong et al. (2025) Three-dimensional electro-thermal coupling analysis of ultra-high-voltage autotransformer based on MPI-PETSc parallel computing framework doi:10.1186/s40807-025-00207-6
results.abstract: Ultra-high-voltage (UHV) autotransformers are widely employed in long-distance power transmission systems. Their operation involves complex energy conversion and coupling mechanisms, including high-intensity magnetic induction energy and strong induced currents. From the perspective of power systems and automation control, it is essential to construct a comprehensive equivalent control circuit for UHV autotransformers, integrating the analysis of induced current and magnetic flux density into the domain of analog electronics. Numerical analysis has become a core approach for investigating the external thermal physical characteristics of transformer power and various thermal management strategies. In this paper, the Message Passing Interface (MPI) and Portable, Extensible Toolkit for Scientific Computation (PETSc) parallel computing framework is adopted to compute and analyze the electro-thermal coupling in a UHV autotransformer. The dielectric loss of transformer components is thoroughly examined. A linear numerical simulation method for evaluating dielectric loss is assessed through parallel computation and validated via the design of a three-dimensional coupling model for leakage flux and core temperature rise. The dielectric loss calculation is applied to the transformer. Magnetostriction measurements under rated output power and various current and voltage conditions reveal the correlation between the coupled data and the thermal topology. The MPI-PETSc framework significantly enhances the computational efficiency of three-dimensional electro-thermal coupling problems in UHV autotransformers through distributed computing and efficient numerical solving, making it suitable for large-scale, high-precision engineering simulations.

results.title: Study on the Electromagnetic Calculations and Failure Mechanisms of Secondary Equipment in High-Voltage Series Compensation Platforms Based on Field-Circuit Coupling
results.citation: Chenye Zhou et al. (2025) Study on the Electromagnetic Calculations and Failure Mechanisms of Secondary Equipment in High-Voltage Series Compensation Platforms Based on Field-Circuit Coupling doi:10.1109/EEPS68057.2025.11351728
results.venue: 2025 5th IEEE International Conference on Energy Engineering and Power Systems (EEPS)
results.abstract: Ultra-High Voltage (UHV) series compensation devices significantly boost power transmission capacity (by 30 % 50 %) and grid stability by reducing line inductive reactance [1]. The secondary equipment on such platforms, acting as the “nervous center” for primary equipment monitoring, control, and protection [1], [2], is critical to operational safety. However, its compact layout ($\leq 1.5 \mathrm{m}$ from primary equipment) exposes it to severe electromagnetic interference (EMI): primary operations (e.g., disconnector switching, spark gap breakdown) generate Fast Transient Over-voltages (FTOVs, up to 140 kV, tens of nanoseconds rise time [2], [3]) that disrupt secondary functions via conduction, radiation, and magnetic coupling. Current research has gaps: simplified 2D models fail to capture 3D electric field distributions [4]; failure mechanism analysis lacks quantification of overvoltage-shielding-failure correlations [5], [6]; and shielding design relies on experience rather than field-based optimization [3]. With EMI-induced secondary equipment failures accounting for $\mathbf{2 3 \% - 3 5 \%}$ of total faults (causing protection misoperation and million-yuan losses per outage [2], [6]), targeted research is urgent. This paper investigates the failure mechanisms and EMI characteristics of UHV series compensation platform secondary equipment under overvoltage. Using the finite element method, a full 3D electromagnetic-structure coupling model is built for shielded and unshielded scenarios. By applying primary system overvoltage signals and analyzing electric field distributions, the equipment's failure behavior and EMI response are examined. The study provides precise support for anti-interference design of high-voltage series compensation platform secondary equipment.

results.title: Monitoring and Simulation of Curing-Induced Residual Strain in Epoxy Core of Ultra-High-Voltage Bushing
results.citation: Yu Zhang et al. (2026) Monitoring and Simulation of Curing-Induced Residual Strain in Epoxy Core of Ultra-High-Voltage Bushing doi:10.3390/en19112718
results.abstract: The UHV dry-type bushing plays a critical role in power transmission by enabling electrical connection, electrical insulation, and mechanical support, making it a core component for ensuring the safe and stable operation of UHV direct current (DC) transmission projects. Epoxy resin, serving as the fundamental insulating material for the bushing core, undergoes significant residual strain during high-temperature curing due to chemical shrinkage and thermal strain, which directly affects the molding quality and service reliability of the component. This paper investigates the curing process of a large-thickness epoxy material, which is on the same scale as a UHV bushing. An in situ monitoring system combining fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensors and thermocouples, together with COMSOL Multiphysics simulations, is employed to systematically study the evolution of the temperature field and residual strain throughout the entire curing process, considering the demolding effect. The results show that during the curing stage, the internal temperature distribution is non-uniform, with a maximum temperature difference of 65 °C between the center and the edge. The residual strain is dominated by chemical shrinkage (accounting for 73.25%) and exhibits a pronounced radial gradient. Mold constraint and demolding cause abrupt changes in the strain. The developed thermo-chemo-mechanical coupled model shows good agreement between simulations and experimental measurements. Thermal cycling relaxes the residual stress, achieving a reduction of 3.89–5.77%. This study provides support for process optimization and defect prevention in large-scale epoxy insulation components.

results.title: Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid
results.citation: Zheng Bowen et al. (2018) Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid doi:10.1109/CICED.2018.8592243
results.venue: China International Conference on Electricity Distribution
results.abstract: With the continuous operation of ultra-high voltage (UHV) AC and DC power transmission and transformation projects in Inner Mongolia autonomous region, Inner Mongolia power grid has become a UHV AC and DC hybrid system. However, the development of AC and DC projects is not balanced, and the characteristics of “strong DC and weak AC” are obvious, It is bound to affect the safe and stable operation of UHV power grids, when sending end matching thermal power units connecting to UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, the reliability of power supply is difficult to be guaranteed. Taking the sending end regional power grid of Xilinhot to Taizhou (Jiangsu Province) UHV DC transmission project (hereinafter referred to as Xitai DC) as the research object, by using Power System Analysis Software Package simulation software, this paper deeply analyzes the influence of high-power matching power supply on the safe and stable operation of the power grid after connecting to the UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, and put forward the corresponding prevention and control measures.

results.title: Spare Parts Management Strategy for Seismic Resilience Improvement of Ultra-high Voltage Converter Station Converter Station
results.citation: Zhihang Xue et al. (2024) Spare Parts Management Strategy for Seismic Resilience Improvement of Ultra-high Voltage Converter Station Converter Station doi:10.1109/ACFPE63443.2024.10800757
results.venue: 2024 3rd Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
results.abstract: Improving resilience of power system is an important aspect of smart grid research. Ultra-high voltage (UHV) converter station serves as core node for power grid and appropriate spare parts can significantly shorten post-earthquake repair time of the converter station and therefore enhance its seismic resilience. This article takes spare parts strategy of the UHV converter station under budget constraint as its research object. Firstly, a functional component model of the UHV converter station is established, and post-earthquake transmission function indicator and seismic resilience index R with per-unit characteristics are defined. Subsequently, the two-stage stochastic programming (TSSP) method was employed to construct a two-stage optimization model for the seismic resilience improvement of the UHV converter station. The sample average approximation (SAA) method was used to solve the model. Lastly, the effectiveness of the proposed method was validated using data from the ± 800kV UHV converter station.

results.title: Audio Detection and Fault Diagnosis of Ultra-High Voltage Transformers
results.citation: Wu Dong et al. (2024) Audio Detection and Fault Diagnosis of Ultra-High Voltage Transformers doi:10.1109/ICSADL61749.2024.00124
results.venue: 2024 3rd International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
results.abstract: This research work addresses the critical issue of audio detection and fault diagnosis in Ultra High Voltage (UHV) transformers, essential components of power transmission systems. Various faults such as short-circuits, insulation problems and oil temperature anomalies can have a significant impact on power converters and the entire power grid. This study proposes a novel approach using audio signals for real-time fault detection and diagnosis. The proposed methodology involves the extraction of audio signals from ultra-high voltage transformers, taking into account factors such as peak frequency, frequency offset, and total harmonic distortion (THD). A deep neural network is used for systematic analysis and interpretation of these features to enable accurate fault detection. Moreover, this study presents a thorough verification process using an experimental dataset, demonstrating superior detection accuracy compared to existing models. The dataset includes diverse samples of normal and fault scenarios, and the proposed model consistently achieves accuracy levels between 98% and 100%. The results suggest the effectiveness of the proposed audio-based methodology for enabling reliable fault detection in ultrahigh-voltage transformers.

results.title: Research on the Influence of Three phase Voltage Imbalance on the Low Voltage Side of Ultra high Voltage Single phase Transformers
results.citation: Tao Tong et al. (2024) Research on the Influence of Three phase Voltage Imbalance on the Low Voltage Side of Ultra high Voltage Single phase Transformers doi:10.1109/AEES63781.2024.10872613
results.venue: 2024 IEEE 5th International Conference on Advanced Electrical and Energy Systems (AEES)
results.abstract: In order to study the effect of the capacitance difference of single-phase ultra-high voltage (UHV) transformer windings on the three-phase voltage imbalance of low-voltage ungrounded systems, an equivalent model of the power and load characteristics of electrical equipment such as transformers, compensation devices, and measurement devices was established by decoupling the high and medium voltage windings of transformers from the grounding capacity; Comparative analysis of the impact of three-phase voltage imbalance in windings at different positions on ungrounded systems. The results indicate that the voltage balance on the low voltage side mainly depends on the capacitance difference between the main transformer and the low voltage winding, as well as the operating mode of the low voltage side continuously variable transmission. This can be improved by investing in large grounded capacitor continuously variable transmissions or replacing them with continuously variable transmissions of different capacities. Simulation and field experiments have verified the accuracy and practicality of this method.

results.title: Reduction in Noise and Vibration in Ultra-High-Voltage Shunt Reactors Using Structural Optimization and Damping Techniques
results.citation: E. Amitov et al. (2026) Reduction in Noise and Vibration in Ultra-High-Voltage Shunt Reactors Using Structural Optimization and Damping Techniques doi:10.3390/app16104929
results.abstract: This paper presents an effective approach to reducing noise and vibration levels in ultra-high-voltage (UHV) shunt reactors based on structural optimization and damping techniques. The main sources of vibration are associated with magnetostriction of electrical steel and electromagnetic forces in the magnetic system, which induce structural excitation of the reactor tank. A combined numerical and experimental methodology is employed, including finite element modeling (FEM) of the reactor tank and field measurements of vibration displacement and acoustic noise. In contrast to previous studies focused primarily on material properties, this work emphasizes the role of structural modifications in controlling vibration transmission. The proposed solutions include the use of nitrile butadiene rubber (NBR) damping elements, optimization of the magnetic system geometry, and reinforcement of the tank structure using vertical and horizontal stiffeners. The FEM analysis in the frequency range of 50–150 Hz shows that the maximum displacement amplitude reaches 16.2 μm at the tank bottom and 10.5 μm at the tank walls. Experimental results confirm a reduction in vibration levels to 13 μm and a sound power level of 88 dBA, which meets regulatory requirements. The proposed approach improves the vibroacoustic performance and operational reliability of UHV reactors and can be effectively applied in the design of modern high-voltage power equipment.

In contrast to previous studies focused primarily on material properties, this work emphasizes the role of structural modifications in controlling vibration transmission. The proposed solutions include the use of nitrile butadiene rubber (NBR) damping elements, optimization of the magnetic system geometry, and reinforcement of the tank structure using vertical and horizontal stiffeners. The FEM analysis in the frequency range of 50–150 Hz shows that the maximum displacement amplitude reaches 16.2 μm at the tank bottom and 10.5 μm at the tank walls. Experimental results confirm a reduction in vibration levels to 13 μm and a sound power level of 88 dBA, which meets regulatory requirements. The proposed approach improves the vibroacoustic performance and operational reliability of UHV reactors and can be effectively applied in the design of modern high-voltage power equipment.

results.title: N‐Vinylcarbazole Group Grafted Modified Silicone Rubber for Dielectric Performance Improvement of High‐Voltage Insulation
results.citation: Jie Mao et al. (2025) N‐Vinylcarbazole Group Grafted Modified Silicone Rubber for Dielectric Performance Improvement of High‐Voltage Insulation doi:10.1002/app.57757
results.abstract: Silicone rubber serves as a critical insulating material in electrical engineering due to its outstanding antifouling characteristics, mechanical robustness, and electrical performance. Nevertheless, its insulating properties require further enhancement to meet the demanding requirements of ultra‐high voltage (UHV) applications, particularly for long‐distance, high‐capacity power transmission. In this work, we developed an improved insulating silicone rubber by grafting p–p conjugated N‐vinyl carbazole (VK) groups—known for their space charge trapping capability—onto thiol‐silicone oil, followed by cross‐linking with end‐methyl branched vinyl silicone oil. The modified material demonstrated optimal insulating performance at a VK group concentration of 0.5 wt%, achieving a 23.19% increase in breakdown field strength compared to unmodified silicone rubber films. This study provides a viable approach for enhancing the dielectric strength of polymer‐based silicone rubber materials.

results.title: Geomagnetically Induced Current Calculation of High Voltage Power System With Long Transmission Lines Using Kriging Method
results.citation: Yu-hao Chen et al. (2022) Geomagnetically Induced Current Calculation of High Voltage Power System With Long Transmission Lines Using Kriging Method doi:10.1109/TPWRD.2021.3068216
results.abstract: Calculation of geomagnetically induced current (GIC) flowing through power system during the geomagnetic storm has attracted more attention recently. However, for high voltage power systems with transmission lines over hundreds or even thousands of kilometers, the earth model and geomagnetical field generally vary significantly. So, it is essential to take them into consideration using limited earth survey sites and geomagnetic observatories. To address this problem, a Kriging method is introduced in this paper to make earth model and geomagnetical field interpolations. It has the characteristic of spatial autocorrelation by considering not only the distances between predicted points and training points but also the distances between training points themselves. Finally, a case study of the Central China 1000 kV ultra-high voltage (UHV) grid is carried out to illustrate the applicability and effectiveness of the proposed method.

results.title: Attention Mechanism-Based Micro-Terrain Recognition for High-Voltage Transmission Lines
results.citation: Ke Mo et al. (2025) Attention Mechanism-Based Micro-Terrain Recognition for High-Voltage Transmission Lines doi:10.3390/en18174495
results.abstract: With the continuous expansion of power grids and the advancement of ultra-high voltage (UHV) projects, transmission lines are increasingly traversing areas characterized by micro-terrain. These localized topographic features can intensify meteorological effects, thereby increasing the risks of hazards such as conductor icing and galloping, directly threatening operational stability. Enhancing the disaster resilience of transmission lines in such environments requires accurate and efficient terrain identification. However, conventional recognition methods often neglect the spatial alignment of the transmission lines, limiting their effectiveness. This paper proposes a deep learning-based recognition framework that incorporates a dual-branch network architecture and a cross-branch spatial attention mechanism to address this limitation. The model explicitly captures the spatial correlation between transmission lines and surrounding terrain by utilizing line alignment information to guide attention along the line corridor. A semi-synthetic dataset, comprising 6495 simulated samples and 130 real-world samples, was constructed to facilitate model training and evaluation. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves classification accuracies of 94.6% on the validation set and 92.8% on real-world test cases, significantly outperforming conventional baseline methods. These findings demonstrate that explicitly modeling the spatial relationship between transmission lines and terrain features substantially improves recognition accuracy, offering important support for hazard prevention and resilience enhancement in UHV transmission systems.

results.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission
results.citation: Yaru Zhang et al. (2018) System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission doi:10.1063/1.5013177
results.venue: Journal of Renewable and Sustainable Energy
results.abstract: Energy transportation is an important issue for China's electricity system due to the unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands. The traditional method of energy transportation (e.g., coal transportation) is challenging due to both the long distance of transportation and the large amount of energy that it requires. In recent years, China has been developing ultra-high voltage (UHV) as an alternative way of transmitting energy over a long distance, and many UHV lines are being planned for construction during the 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) period. This paper develops a system optimization model for exploring the optimal pathway for long-distance energy transportation in China during the 13th FYP period from the perspective of the minimum cost of an energy transportation system. The results show that (a) although UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted in the 13th FYP period, coal transportation will still play an important role; (b) eight UHV lines are suggested to be built and put into use as a priority in the 13th FYP period; (c) the high technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants would promote the adoption of UHV. Based on these results, some policy suggestions are provided.Energy transportation is an important issue for China's electricity system due to the unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands. results.title: Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering
results.citation: D. Danylchenko et al. (2025) Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering doi:10.20998/eree.2025.1(10).333342
results.venue: Bulletin of the National Technical University "KhPI". Series: Energy: Reliability and Energy Efficiency
results.abstract: The article presents a comprehensive study of China's energy security from a technical and economic perspective, taking into account current challenges and strategic goals of the energy transition. The main focus is on the quantitative analysis of structural problems that threaten the stability of the energy system. In particular, based on 2023 data, it was found that the structure of energy consumption remains dependent on fossil fuels: the share of coal is 56.4 %, and oil import dependence is 72.6 %. In addition, the probability of overloading the power grid in extreme weather conditions was found to be 7.3 %, indicating the system's vulnerability to climate change. The study modeled the effectiveness of the implementation of Smart Grid technologies, which increased the efficiency of ultra-high voltage power transmission by 18 %. The study also recorded a 40 % increase in the total capacity of virtual power plants and an increase in the internal rate of return of energy storage systems based on lithium batteries to 8.7 %. From the perspective of long-term strategic planning, the proposed multi-energy flow model estimates the cost of transporting 500 km of green hydrogen at 35 yuan/kg, and also forms a standardized power compensation mechanism (0.08 yuan/kW). The empirical results confirm the feasibility of such measures: the rate of rejection of new transmission lines in the northwestern regions has been reduced to 3.2 %, and the introduction of 12 GW of energy storage systems in the Yangtze Delta has reduced peak load by 15%. It is also demonstrated that the cost of hydrogen production from offshore wind energy has reached 0.3 yuan/kWh, or 35 yuan/kg, taking into account market electricity tariffs. The article proposes strategic measures to ensure energy security, including investments in scientific and technological development in the amount of 0.8 % of gross domestic product, modernization of the power grid with funding of 4.2 trillion yuan over the next 10 years, and the creation of an adaptive political monitoring system. These results form the basis for a systematic electrical engineering approach to ensuring China's energy sustainability in the context of global transformations.

results.title: Active Power Coordinated Control of Grid-Forming Renewable Generations and DC Transmission System
results.citation: Zhi Jing et al. (2024) Active Power Coordinated Control of Grid-Forming Renewable Generations and DC Transmission System doi:10.1109/ICPEE64457.2024.10950001
results.venue: 2024 8th International Conference on Power and Energy Engineering (ICPEE)
results.abstract: In recent years, the large-scale wind and photovoltaic power bases have been actively promoted in desert and arid regions, with the construction of ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission channels. However, faults in the DC transmission channels of these new energy bases can lead to short-term active power fluctuations, potentially causing instability in the angle and frequency deviations in the sending-end power system. This paper first establishes a model of a wind-solar power base under ideal conditions, where the wind power system employs a network-forming control strategy to enhance grid connection stability, while the photovoltaic system continues to use the traditional grid-following control. Through theoretical analysis of the spatiotemporal distribution characteristics of frequency in the new energy power system, the paper explores the factors influencing these distribution characteristics, discusses the necessity of studying these characteristics, and develops a coordinated energy flow control strategy for the wind-solar power base. Simulation results verify the accuracy of the proposed frequency indices and the effectiveness of the control strategy, summarizing the frequency variation patterns considering spatiotemporal distribution effects.

results.title: Comparative assessment of fault detection methods in transmission line during power swing: analyzing single and hybrid networks for the southern Kerala grid
results.citation: S. K et al. (2024) Comparative assessment of fault detection methods in transmission line during power swing: analyzing single and hybrid networks for the southern Kerala grid doi:10.1088/2631-8695/ad3895
results.abstract: The blocking function of power oscillation is a highly effective feature found in distance relays. Its purpose is to prevent unintentional tripping of transmission lines. It is crucial to accurately differentiate between faults and power oscillations to minimise the occurrence of widespread power outages and monetary losses. Therefore, the detection and classification of faults arising from power swings are critical challenges in ensuring the smooth operation and overall health of transmission lines, particularly in extra-high-voltage (EHV) and ultra-high-voltage (UHV) power systems. Modern methods rely on machine learning methods which are in their infancy. The paper proposes to identify various fault conditions in transmission lines during power swing by using a hybrid Neural Network architecture. In this research, different designs such as 2-D convolution neural network (2D-CNN), 1-D convolution neural network (1D-CNN), long short-term memory (LSTM), and CNN-LSTM hybrid networks are investigated to understand the effect of various deep learning approaches. Along with the southern Kerala grid, the standard 9-bus system is selected as a test case to examine various fault circumstances of the system during power swing. Multiple tests were conducted to determine the optimal deep learning architecture, including model parameters and configurations, for accurate fault detection. For the 9 bus system, the 1D CNN network performs better with an accuracy of 98.70%, and for the Kerala grid, both networks are competitive, but the CNN-LSTM Hybrid method slightly outperforms the 1D CNN with an accuracy of 92.40%.

results.title: Simplified Risk Assessment of DC Bias for Ultra-High-Voltage Direct-Current Transmission
results.citation: Lu Zhang et al. (2020) Simplified Risk Assessment of DC Bias for Ultra-High-Voltage Direct-Current Transmission doi:10.1109/EI250167.2020.9346824
results.venue: 2020 IEEE 4th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
results.abstract: At present, there are some technical difficulties in the risk assessment of DC bias in the power grid of the UHV project. A simplified assessment method for considering the risk of DC bias in the power grid of the UHV project is proposed in this paper. Firstly, a simple calculation method of earth surface potential is proposed based on the equivalent of point and source of DC grounding electrode surface based on the multi-layered earth model, and on this basis, a bias risk assessment method is proposed that only the power station/DC grounding electrode position and the system resistance network is needed to be provided. By using an calculation example, the calculation accuracy of the simple calculation method of earth surface potential is proved to meet the engineering application requirements, and by considering the DC bias risk assessment calculated-example of the Hebei South Network of the UHV project, the practicality of this method in the early warning and management of DC bias risk is proved.

results.title: Study on the Influence of Large-scale New Energy Connect to the Operation Characteristics of Ultra-high Voltage Sending end Network with Weak Synchronization Support
results.citation: Bowen Zheng et al. (2021) Study on the Influence of Large-scale New Energy Connect to the Operation Characteristics of Ultra-high Voltage Sending end Network with Weak Synchronization Support doi:10.1109/CEEPE51765.2021.9475769
results.venue: 2021 4th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
results.abstract: In recent years, new energy sources such as wind power and photovoltaic have entered a period of rapid development at home and abroad. The unbalanced distribution of energy and load determines the main characteristics of large-scale development and centralized delivery of new energy in China. Therefore, large capacity and long-distance UHV transmission technology has become an important carrier of intensive and efficient development and large-scale allocation and consumption of clean energy. However, due to its characteristics of weak inertia, large output fluctuation and poor reactive power support, new energy seriously affects the safe and stable operation of the power grid, especially for the sending end grid with weak synchronization support, the large-scale access of new energy will have a inestimable impact on the transmission capacity and operation characteristics of UHV, and the reliability of power supply is difficult to be guaranteed. In this paper, PSASP (power system analysis software package) simulation software is used to study the transmission end power grid of Ximeng UHV AC / DC transmission project, the simulation analysis of the system strength, stability level, power transmission capacity and other operation characteristics of the power grid in the region after the planned new energy access is carried out, the impact of large-scale new energy on the safe and stable operation of UHV AC-DC hybrid power grid is analyzed in depth, and the corresponding effective prevention and control measures are proposed.

results.title: Research on coordinated control strategy for active power output of energy storage systems based on multi‑infeed UHV and CIGRE DC standard test model
results.citation: Qianchang Chen et al. (2026) Research on coordinated control strategy for active power output of energy storage systems based on multi‑infeed UHV and CIGRE DC standard test model doi:10.1117/12.3108666
results.venue: International Conference on Physics, Photonics, and Optical Engineering
results.abstract: In the development of modern social construction, the application scope of high-voltage direct current transmission projects is becoming increasingly wide. However, the DC power input from outside the power grid is overly concentrated and powerful, while the AC network for local digestion and distribution of this power is relatively fragile and unable to effectively buffer and regulate. Therefore, problems such as poor stability, difficult peak shaving, and difficult voltage control are prone to occur. It directly affects the safety of AC and DC power grid construction in the new era. This paper studies, after understanding various methods for suppressing commutation failure, clarifies the mechanism of the active power output of energy storage on the commutation conditions of AC power grids based on the research results proposed by existing scholars. Then, simulation experiments and analyses are conducted using the multi input ultra-high voltage direct current transmission model in a certain area and the CIGRE DC standard test model. Finally, the coordinated control strategy of the active power output of energy storage is clarified. It is effective in suppressing subsequent commutation failures.

results.title: Research on the vibration detection technology of ultra high voltage oil immersed shunt reactor based on voiceprint-infrared fusion
results.citation: Z. Gu et al. (2023) Research on the vibration detection technology of ultra high voltage oil immersed shunt reactor based on voiceprint-infrared fusion doi:10.1117/12.2681341
results.venue: Conference on Image, Signal Processing, and Pattern Recognition
results.abstract: Oil immersed shunt reactor is one of the most important reactive power compensation equipment in Ultra high voltage (UHV) long-distance power transmission and transformation system. When the oil immersed shunt reactor has potential defects, the vibration signal during operation will be different from that under normal condition, which is shown as different defect signals such as noise and abnormal temperature. Compared with traditional vibration sensor detection, acoustic detection and infrared detection realize non-contact detection, which is safer and does not require complex vibration sensor layout. If the two are combined, the detection efficiency can be greatly improved.In this paper, the acoustic characteristics and thermal distribution characteristics of oil immersed shunt reactors under different vibration modes are studied. Based on the beam forming sound source space positioning calculation method, after the integration of voiceprint and infrared image is completed, the acoustic imaging and infrared detection technology of reactor vibration is proposed to realize the simultaneous monitoring of reactor sound temperature state information, and improve the accuracy and rationality of defect identification. In addition, the design scheme of a prototype for on-site acoustic infrared detection of reactor vibration is proposed. The related results of this paper can provide technical support for the application of UHV oil immersed shunt reactor state assessment and diagnosis, and improve the level of equipment operation and maintenance.

results.title: Welding technology of ultra high voltage transmission steel pipe tower butt welded joint by MIG welding
results.citation: Hanyang et al. (2018) Welding technology of ultra high voltage transmission steel pipe tower butt welded joint by MIG welding doi:10.1088/1755-1315/199/5/052002
results.venue: IOP Conference Series: Earth and Environment
results.abstract: In twenty-first Century, China vigorously promoted the research and construction of AC and DC transmission technology in order to ensure the optimal allocation of energy resources in a large scale[1]. The transmission line is the core of the power grid, which plays the role of transmission, transformation and distribution of electric energy, and each tower is the core component of the transmission line. At present, because the force of the steel tube tower is more reasonable, it has been widely used in the construction of UHV, and the link of the key position of the steel tube tower is welded, so the quality of the welding is of great significance to the construction of the whole network frame. For the welding of the steel tube tower, the manual semi automatic CO2 gas shielded welding is mostly used. Because of the characteristics of the protective gas medium, it is easy to produce a large number of splashes and not easy to clean up during the welding process. It is easy to make the weld penetration rate and forming decline. It is effective to use the melting pole mixture gas protection welding, If we can make a scientific welding standard, it is possible to reduce the occurrence of welding spatter, achieve the purpose of improving welding efficiency and controlling manufacturing cost.

results.title: Characteristics of very fast transient currents in ultra high-voltage power system with Hybrid reactive power compensation
results.citation: Hongshun Liu et al. (2018) Characteristics of very fast transient currents in ultra high-voltage power system with Hybrid reactive power compensation doi:10.1016/J.IJEPES.2018.06.034
results.venue: International Journal of Electrical Power & Energy Systems
results.abstract: Abstract Hybrid reactive power compensation (HRPC) consists of a stepped controlled shunt reactor (SCSR) and a series compensation (SC), which will find applications in future ultra high-voltage (UHV) power grids to resolve the problems due to the frequent change of reactive power and bulk power transmission. However, very fast transient currents (VFTCs) are inevitably generated during switching, which would lead to insulation breakdown. In the present work, we first develop the equivalent model for HRPC, following which we deduce the expression of VFTCs in the time domain by using an inverse Laplace transform. The analysis indicates that the amplitude and frequency of VFTCs are both affected by the capacitance of the SCSR and of the SC, as well as the line length, stray capacitance, etc. The oscillating frequency, peak, and amplitude of the main frequency of the VFTCs in the substation can be modified by adjusting the silicon-controlled rectifiers in the SCSR when the disconnecting switch in gas-insulated switchgear is switched on. When the disconnecting switch in the SC is switched on, the VFTC oscillation frequency in SCSR decreases with increasing stray capacitance of SC, but the frequency and peak of the VFTC remains quite large. Increasing the line length between the SC and the SCSR suppresses the VFTC in UHV power systems. These results lay the foundation for developing HRPC methods to suppress VFTCs in UHV substations.

results.title: Coordinated Interactive Optimization Model of Multiple Green Power Entities for UHV Supporting New Energy
results.citation: Xie Xu et al. (2025) Coordinated Interactive Optimization Model of Multiple Green Power Entities for UHV Supporting New Energy doi:10.1109/CITSC64390.2025.00103
results.venue: Conference on Innovative Trends in Computer Science
results.abstract: Aiming at the characteristics of ultra-high voltage supporting new energy power grid, this paper proposes a coordinated interactive optimization model of multiple green power entities. Based on the principle of game theory, this model constructs a multi-objective optimization framework to realize the coordinated dispatch of various green power entities in the inter-provincial transmission network. The model solves the dynamic game among multiple entities, and optimizes the power allocation strategy of each entity under the conditions of power balance constraints, power flow constraints, voltage constraints and line transmission capacity constraints to ensure the safe and stable operation of the power grid. This paper first analyzes the relationship between ultra-high voltage power grid and new energy supporting, establishes mathematical models of wind power clusters, photovoltaic clusters and energy storage, and clarifies the coordinated interaction mechanism between various entities. Subsequently, this paper proposes a collaborative optimization algorithm based on Nash equilibrium, and discusses in detail the solution process and its application in the multi-objective model. Finally, the effectiveness of the model is verified through the simulation analysis of the IEEE39 node system. The simulation results show that the proposed model can significantly improve the economic benefits of the system and the utilization rate of new energy, while effectively reducing the operating cost and network loss of the system.

results.title: Study on Human Exposure to Power Frequency Electric Field under UHV Transmission Line
results.citation: Liao Ying et al. (2022) Study on Human Exposure to Power Frequency Electric Field under UHV Transmission Line doi:10.1109/icmee56406.2022.10093549
results.venue: International Conference on Mechanical and Electronics Engineering
results.abstract: The development, construction and research of Ultra High Voltage transmission system has become the top priority of China's power industry. When the voltage transmitted by the transmission line reaches the ultra-high voltage stage, the power frequency electric field intensity around the transmission line will also increase, which may cause electromagnetic compatibility or electrical safety problems, thus having a certain impact on the human body. In order to meet the requirements of China's electromagnetic environment around transmission lines, it is necessary to study the electromagnetic environment around UHV transmission lines. In this context, this paper studies the distribution of power frequency electric field and human body current under the electromagnetic environment of UHV AC transmission lines by finite element analysis. The human body model is established by using three-dimensional finite element analysis software ANSYS to study how to set reasonable human electrical parameters in the modeling process. The induced current of human body in power frequency electric field is qualitatively analyzed, and the influence of skin effect is analyzed. 1000 kV UHV AC transmission line is established by ANSYS software. The power frequency electric field distribution under the transmission line is simulated, and the electric field distribution law under the UHV AC transmission line is obtained. The human body model is placed under the transmission line to simulate the electric field intensity and current density distribution on the human body surface and around the transmission line. The influence of human body on the electric field distribution under transmission lines is compared and analyzed. According to the relevant power frequency electric field limit standard and the electric field distribution under the UHV transmission line obtained by simulation, the safety zone under the transmission line is proposed.

results.title: Coordinated Transient-Voltage Support by a Dual-Mode Flexible Switching Strategy for Grid-Tied Distributed PV-Battery Converters
results.citation: Zhenliang Nie et al. (2025) Coordinated Transient-Voltage Support by a Dual-Mode Flexible Switching Strategy for Grid-Tied Distributed PV-Battery Converters doi:10.1109/EI268505.2025.11425110
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
results.abstract: As the scale of ultra-high-voltage (UHV) AC/DC hybrid transmission keeps expanding, large-scale bulk-power infeed and local renewable integration displace conventional synchronous generation in receiving-end grids, eroding system strength and resulting in weak-grid characteristics with low short-circuit ratios. Traditional single-mode grid-following or gridforming converters can no longer fully satisfy grid requirements. Taking the distributed PV-battery grid-tied converter as the research object, this paper analyzes the inherent control principles of grid-following and grid-forming converters and their respective voltage-support capabilities. A grid-friendly dual-mode flexible switching control strategy for distributed PV-battery systems is proposed. By establishing quantitative evaluation indices and performing PSCAD/EMTDC simulation, the proposed strategy is compared with conventional single-mode controls under identical disturbances. The impacts of proximate and remote connections are also examined. Results verify that the proposed scheme significantly enhances grid transient-voltage stability.

results.title: Prediction of Corona Losses in UHV AC Transmission Lines under Varying Temperature Conditions
results.citation: Jialei Shi (2026) Prediction of Corona Losses in UHV AC Transmission Lines under Varying Temperature Conditions doi:10.54254/2755-2721/2026.bj32510
results.abstract: The accurate prediction of power losses in ultra-high-voltage (UHV) AC transmission systems is critical for ensuring the economic and efficient operation of modern power grids. This paper investigates the temperature-dependent fluctuations in line losses—particularly those induced by corona discharge—under varying ambient conditions. To this end, this paper proposes a comprehensive loss-prediction framework integrating Spearman rank correlation analysis with a particle swarm optimization–enhanced extra-trees (PSO-ET) model. The methodology is validated using operational data from 1000 kV UHV transmission projects in the Fujian–Zhejiang region. First, Spearman correlation analysis confirms temperature as the dominant meteorological factor influencing line losses. Subsequently, the PSO algorithm is employed to globally optimize three key hyperparameters of the ET model: tree count, maximum tree depth, and the humidity threshold used to dichotomize operating conditions into "dry" and "high-humidity" regimes. This enables precise decoupled training under distinct environmental states. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an average absolute error (MAE) of only 5.1315 MW on the independent test set. Further sensitivity analysis reveals that the gradient of line loss with respect to temperature peaks at approximately 7.6 °C—indicating a pronounced nonlinear response. Crucially, this inflection coincides with the onset of surface condensation, thereby uncovering a previously underappreciated surge effect: in low-temperature, high-humidity environments, condensation on conductor surfaces significantly amplifies corona losses. This study provides a robust, data-driven foundation for real-time loss monitoring and energy-efficient scheduling of UHV transmission lines under complex and dynamic meteorological conditions.

results.title: Technical and Economic Analysis of ACCC Applied in UHV-DC Power Transmission Lines
results.citation: Li Zhou et al. (2019) Technical and Economic Analysis of ACCC Applied in UHV-DC Power Transmission Lines doi:10.1109/IGBSG.2019.8886199
results.venue: International Conference on Intelligent Green Building and Smart Grid
results.abstract: In order to improve the efficient and safe of transmission lines to transmit electrical energy, new energy-saving type wires have emerged. Among them, the aluminum conductor with reinforced core of carbon fiber (ACCC) wire has the advantages of high current carrying capacity, low sag, corrosion resistance, high tensile strength, etc., and is economically effective in the transmission line. However, it is mainly used in the capacity expansion and transformation of the power grid, and the economics of its application in Ultra high voltage (UHV) DC transmission line needs to be further studied. In this paper, the annual cost method is used to analyze the electrical performance, mechanical properties and economy of ACCC and conventional aluminum conductor steel-reinforced (ACSR) wire. The life cycle cost of two wires when erected at various possible spans of 100 meters are obtained. It provides a theoretical basis for the application of ACCC in UHV DC transmission lines.

results.title: Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems
results.citation: Lei Fu et al. (2025) Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11089103
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
results.abstract: The revision of China’s national standard GB/T 22384 addresses critical challenges posed by new-type power grids, characterized by large-scale renewable energy integration, ultra-high voltage AC/DC transmission, and cyber-physical system complexities. Aligned with China’s "dual carbon" strategy and the technical demands of New Power Systems, this revision introduces expanded testing scopes, enhanced network security protection requirements, and refined dynamic simulation methodologies. A case study of the Qing-Yu HVDC project demonstrates the effectiveness of closed-loop test based on real-time digital simulation (RTDS) in validating hierarchical security and stability control systems (SSCS), revealing latent defects such as redundant tripping commands during cascading faults. Innovations lie in integrating RTDS for closed-loop validation of multi-scenario strategies, introducing dynamic state synchronization to mitigate coordination failures, and optimizing lifecycle testing management. The revised standard establishes a robust framework for standardized testing, ensuring reliability and adaptability of SSCS in high-renewable, power electronics-dominated grids. From the main aspects of this revision and the results of engineering empirical tests, the testing methods and technical requirements for SSCS should evolve iteratively in sync with the development process and technological advancements of power systems. SSCS plays a crucial role in ensuring the secure and stable operation of power systems, and the standardization work cannot be achieved just in one effort.

results.citation: M. Waqas et al. (2024) A MEMS Based Three Electrodes Ionization Sensor for Real-Time Detection of Transient Overvoltage in UHV Transmission Systems doi:10.1109/APET63768.2024.10882769
results.venue: 2024 3rd Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
results.abstract: Direct Current (HVDC) lines have become critical enablers. However, the intense static electric fields generated by HVDC transmission disrupt normal activities. Hence, it is essential to monitor the fast transient electric fields in ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission lines. Traditional capacitive sensors rely on mechanical motion to convert direct current (DC) electric fields into alternating current (AC) signals, but they suffer from parameter drift and accuracy loss due to long-term operation and nanomaterial degradation. We propose a novel MEMS device for direct, high-resolution measurement of electric field strength with minimal distortion. This device features an array of silicon microcolumn cathodes, a collecting electrode, and an extracting electrode. When subjected to an external e-field, free charge carriers within the discharge space allow the sensitive nanomaterial to extract charge in response to electric field intensity, leading to charge redistribution and polarization among the electrodes. This induced field strength causes ionization, generating charged particles displaced by opposing electrical forces ($E_{1}$ and $E_{2}$), which are collected in the collector groove and are directly proportional to electric field intensity. This highly integrated sensor array can detect even small transient electric fields, offering a promising solution for power system and environmental safety.

results.title: Full Electromagnetic TransientFine Modeling and Characteristics Study of AC/DC Hybrid Power Grid with High Ratio of Renewable Energy
results.citation: Lixiang Sun et al. (2023) Full Electromagnetic TransientFine Modeling and Characteristics Study of AC/DC Hybrid Power Grid with High Ratio of Renewable Energy doi:10.1109/ICPET59380.2023.10367526
results.venue: 2023 5th International Conference on Power and Energy Technology (ICPET)
results.abstract: The rapid development of renewable energy and ultra-high voltage AC/DC hybrid power grids has put forward higher requirements for simulation accuracy. Only by accurately simulating the characteristics of renewable energy and DC in the power grid, the response of the power grid to faults cant be accurately reflected. In the past, digital simulations of large-scale power grids were limited by the ability of simulation tools, and renewable energy sources engineering models rarely used or did not use, resulting in simulation accuracy issues. This article proposes an engineering fine modeling method for renewable energy and DC systems. Based on the characteristics of engineering fine modeling for renewable energy and DC systems, and relying on the ADPSS rapid modeling tool, a full electromagnetic transient modeling method for large-scale AC DC hybrid power grids containing high proportion of renewable energy is studied, which solves the problem of difficult modeling for renewable energy and DC systems. Finally, the proposed full electromagnetic transient fast modeling method for AC/DC hybrid power grid with high proportion of renewable energy was applied to construct an electromagnetic transient model for a high proportion of renewable energy transmission system. The influence of DC faults and different outputs of renewable energy on the overvoltage of the transmission end power grid was studied, and the accuracy and effectiveness of the proposed method were verified, this provides a more accurate simulation method for further in-depth research on the interaction between renewable energy and AC/DC in large-scale power grids.

results.title: Influencing Factors of the Power Fluctuation on the Ultra High Voltage Transmission Line Caused by Faults at the Remote Ends of the Interconnected Grid
results.citation: Z. An et al. (2014) Influencing Factors of the Power Fluctuation on the Ultra High Voltage Transmission Line Caused by Faults at the Remote Ends of the Interconnected Grid doi:10.4236/JPEE.2014.24084
results.abstract: After the North China grid and the Central China grid get into connection with the UHVAC demonstration, a new phenomenon is discovered according to some simulations. That is, the faults at the remote end of the UHV interconnected grid will result in significant power fluctuation and voltage drop on the UHV transmission line and even system splitting. But the faults near the UHV line only have marginal effects. Further, the simulation results also indicate that the short-circuit current of the buses near the UHV line is larger than that of the buses far away from the UHV line. This phenomenon is divergent from the traditional view. In this paper, the detail will be introduced, and the factors influencing the system stability after faults are presented and analyzed. The results indicate that transmission power of the UHV line and of the lines between the remote end and the major grid influence the fluctuation on UHV line. The load model and the grid structure of the remote end also have effect on it. Finally, corresponding control scheme is presented to improve the operation conditions of the UHV interconnected grid and ensure its security and stability.

results.title: Reliability Evaluation of Weak Transmission Terminal Power Grid with EHV/UHV Direct Current
results.citation: Tianshu Zhang et al. (2019) Reliability Evaluation of Weak Transmission Terminal Power Grid with EHV/UHV Direct Current doi:10.1109/ICEI.2019.00033
results.venue: 2019 IEEE International Conference on Energy Internet (ICEI)
results.abstract: Nowadays, renewable energy such as wind energy continues developing rapidly. However, renewable energy generation has intermittent and random operating characteristics, and most of China's energy bases are in the northwest, resulting in weak AC networks. At the same time, Extra/ Ultra high voltage direct current transmission will play an important role in multi-area electric power system in China. Therefore, it is urgent to study the reliability of weak transmission terminal power grid with EHV/UHV direct current. Firstly, a generation reliability assessment model for wind farm is built, meanwhile, a simple but effective wake effect model is also proposed. Secondly, the frequency and duration method based on Markov process theory is used for the equivalent to the HVDC transmission system, and its state transition diagram is obtained. Then, the above model is added to the IEEE reliability test system, and the simulation calculation is performed for the system in MATLAB based on the sequential Monte Carlo method. Finally, in view of the results, this paper analyzed the effect of different situations on the system reliability, including different wind speed and wind speed fluctuation, different wind farm site, different wind farm capacity, and different proportion of wind power instead of conventional energy. The reliability of high voltage AC transmission and HVDC transmission is also compared and analyzed.

results.title: Study on Reactive Power Response Characteristics of Grid-Forming Renewable Energy Systems with Virtual Internal Potential
results.citation: Xiaofei Sun et al. (2024) Study on Reactive Power Response Characteristics of Grid-Forming Renewable Energy Systems with Virtual Internal Potential doi:10.1109/APPEEC61255.2024.10922351
results.venue: IEEE PES Asia-Pacific Power and Energy Engineering Conference
results.abstract: The wind energy resources and load distribution in China are inversely correlated. To meet the demand for long-distance power transmission from large-scale clean energy bases in desert areas, ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission lines have become a strategic choice for the development of China's power grid. However, the lack of conventional synchronous power support in the AC sending-end system poses challenges when Commutation Failures or DC Blocking occur at the DC receiving-end, leading to transient overvoltage issues. These problems can result in large-scale disconnection of wind turbines, thereby limiting the ability of the sending-end grid to integrate renewable energy. Suppressing transient overvoltage in the sending-end grid imposes higher requirements on the reactive power support response speed of renewable energy sources. Therefore, this paper investigates the reactive power response characteristics of grid-forming renewable energy systems. It analyzes the influence of various factors such as different control coefficients, voltage measurement delays, and current loop control parameters on the reactive power response characteristics of grid-forming converters. This research provides theoretical support for the design of control strategies, parameter tuning, and capacity configuration of grid-forming renewable energy systems.

results.title: Data governance method for unmanned aerial vehicle inspection for power grid equipment management
results.citation: Sijia Zheng et al. (2024) Data governance method for unmanned aerial vehicle inspection for power grid equipment management doi:10.1088/1742-6596/3036/1/012003
results.venue: 2024 6th International Conference on Applied Machine Learning (ICAML)
results.abstract: Power inspection is gradually shifting towards digitization and intelligence, which puts higher requirements on the quality of basic data. In the power production process, the generated data primarily originates from equipment status and maintenance activities. The suboptimal quality of data from these aspects leads to inadequate performance in analytical applications. To address this issue, this paper proposes a UAV inspection data governance method for power grid equipment management. The method involves acquiring UAV inspection data, including basic records and line video data of ultra-high voltage transmission lines, extracting attribute features of equipment from the videos, and comparing them with the equipment records. A data governance approach based on feature engineering is designed, incorporating data verification algorithms for equipment attribute completeness, topological connectivity, nameplate compliance, and measurement consistency. This enables the validation of maintenance data by integrating real-time measurement data. For missing or erroneous information already present in the system, data governance identifies the root causes, supplements missing data, and corrects errors, thereby resolving inconsistencies across information systems. This method provides a solution framework for enhancing the quality of power grid equipment management data and supporting subsequent analytical applications.

results.title: Research and analysis on 3D visualization simulation of digital twin models for UHV power bushing equipment
results.citation: Yongshen He et al. (2023) Research and analysis on 3D visualization simulation of digital twin models for UHV power bushing equipment doi:10.1117/12.3006787
results.venue: International Conference on Modelling, Identification and Control
results.abstract: Ultra high voltage power bushings are widely used in power systems. In the operation and maintenance process of the ultra high voltage power bushings, testing and analysis are generally based on the equipment itself. If data is obtained from the equipment itself, there will be incomplete data transmission, and the obtained data is generally voltage, current, temperature and other sensing data that are easily obtained from outside power equipment. But for the internal data of power equipment, including online data information such as flow velocity field and pressure field. Therefore, this article attempts to establish the three-dimensional visual simulation model for the digital twin of ultra-high voltage power casing equipment, and further sets key tracking nodes on the three-dimensional configuration model to quantitatively track its multi-dimensional physical quantity change curves and trends. First of all, 3D modeling of large terrain of electric equipment is carried out. Virtual texture technology is used to block and fuse large area and multi resolution texture data. Different detail Hierarchical database model are used to block large area terrain, including texture image selection, ground surface background map selection, texture coverage and grid division. After determining the morphology of the terrain, digital twin 3D modeling analysis was immediately conducted on the power equipment. In the 3D digital twin modeling, the converter transformer body and converter transformer bushing are taken as examples for analysis and explanation. The detailed structure of bushing equipment is considered in the twin model. At the same time, the electrical, thermal and mechanical Multi-physics simulation of converter transformer bushing are coupled and analyzed. Multi-physics simulation simulation results are associated with the key nodes in the 3D digital twin model to realize interaction of physical quantity information between the simulation model and the twin model. The research results of this paper have a good guiding role for the digital operation and maintenance of power equipment, and creatively integrate visual image processing and digital twin Technological convergence to interact, forming a visual simulation research method

results.title: National Strategy and Industrial Governance Shift: Transformation of the Power Transmission and Distribution Industry in China
results.citation: Ying Ma (2016) National Strategy and Industrial Governance Shift: Transformation of the Power Transmission and Distribution Industry in China doi:10.1353/chn.2016.0009
results.abstract: During the past decades, there has been a shift in the governance structure of the power transmission and distribution (PTD) industry from a market-oriented scheme to vertical integration. Such a shift in industrial governance was driven by the change in national strategy from “market in exchange for technology” to “independent innovation”. Under this new policy paradigm, the ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission technology was perceived as strategically important for the country. The State Grid Corporation of China (SGCC), whose control over the industry was initially reduced in the reform, leveraged the paradigm shift in the state policy and strengthened its position in the industry by redefining the main and auxiliary services. The State Grid successfully brought back all UHV industry-related services, such as power design, equipment manufacturing, power grid construction and capital operation, into its main business category, which could be exempted from market competition according to state regulation. Eventually, the State Grid was able to establish strong vertical integration in the whole sector.

results.title: Overview of Main Electrical Equipment for UHVDC Transmission
results.citation: Xiangyu Meng (2025) Overview of Main Electrical Equipment for UHVDC Transmission doi:10.5220/0014320200004918
results.venue: Proceedings of the 3rd International Conference on Computer Science and Mechatronics
results.abstract: : Ultra-high Voltage Direct Current (UHVDC) transmission is the mainstream solution of modern remote power transmission. Converter from the 12 pulse converter mainly using thyristors to the series capacitor converter, and finally developed to the converter using fully controlled devices. The former uses thyristor devices, and transmission-line system of electric power provide the commutation voltage to complete the commutation, the latter is composed of fully controlled devices, and the commutation is completed by the self-turn-off characteristics of the devices. Transmission line protection is mainly divided into traveling wave protection, differential undervoltage protection and longitudinal differential protection. This study conducts a comparative evaluation and systematic categorization of the principal protection methodologies utilized in transmission line systems, with a focus on delineating their respective merits, limitations, and distinguishing operational characteristics under varying fault scenarios. This paper introduces the converter and transmission protection technology from the aspects of circuit structure, principle, advantages and disadvantages. The final section of this research synthesizes key findings while delineating emerging trajectories for next-generation ultra-high-voltage (UHV) power transmission systems, with particular emphasis on their transformative potential in enabling large-scale renewable energy integration and transcontinental grid interoperability.

results.title: Research status of the external insulation pollution flashover of high voltage transmission line
results.citation: Kai Guo et al. (2021) Research status of the external insulation pollution flashover of high voltage transmission line doi:10.1088/1755-1315/651/2/022082
results.abstract: In recent years, China’s electric power industry has made great progress, especially the achievements of ultra high voltage and ultra high voltage power transmission and transformation technology have attracted worldwide attention. At present, China has put into operation power transmission projects such as ±660kV yin-East DC extra-high voltage, ±800kV upward, Yunnan-Guangzhou DC extra-high voltage and 1000kV Dongnan Jindong-Jingmen AC extra-high voltage. These long-distance and large-capacity power transmission projects provide strong technical support for the realization of the national strategic goal of “west power transmission to the east and North power transmission to the south”.With the rapid industrialization and environmental deterioration, various industrial and natural pollutions deposited on the insulators of high-voltage transmission lines are becoming increasingly serious. In severe weather, such as fog, dew, drizzle and snowmelt, the external insulation strength will be greatly reduced. Therefore, it is very important and necessary to thoroughly study the pollution flasking of uHV and UHV insulators and comprehensively consider the influence of China’s complex geographical environment on their external insulation characteristics, which is one of the key issues related to the safe and stable operation of power grid.

results.title: Editorial: Advanced technologies for electrical engineering
results.citation: J. Zha et al. (2022) Editorial: Advanced technologies for electrical engineering doi:10.3389/fmats.2022.1024386
results.abstract: Energy crisis and environmental pollution are two major themes currently faced by human society. The construction of a strong smart grid with super/ultra-high voltage network as the backbone and clean energy transmission as the leading shows a more important strategic role than ever since. However, the rapid development of clean and renewable energy technologies represented by wind and solar energy and the continuous growth of load demand put forward higher requirements for the safe operation of power systems, the guarantee of power supply quality, and conversion and utilization efficiency of electric energy. The performance of electrical equipment based on traditional electrical materials is often limited by their electromagnetic parameters, which can not meet the needs of the power system anymore under the new situation. Based on the above purpose, this special issue was established. To our relief, five papers were accepted in 9 months. These five papers not only put forward new research ideas on traditional problems, but also introduce new materials and new technologies. Polytrifluorovinyl chloride (PCTFE) is one of the earliest fluorinated plastics synthesized and commercialized. It has very low dielectric constant and loss, coupled with good resistance to water vapor penetration, corrosion, light transmission, creep resistance and other excellent properties. Aiming to provide guidance for the future research and application of PCTFE-based materials, Zou et al. reviewed the history of the development and the research progress of PCTFE, and finally proposed the perspective (Zou et al.). There has been increasing interest in applying PCTFE-based materials to energy applications such as piezoelectric and electrostrictive devices, fuel cell membranes, and photovoltaic power generation. It can be expected that these areas will be further explored and will greatly attract the interest of industrial and academic researchers. XLPE power cables are widely used around the world for their excellent transmission stability and economy, as well as being laid underground without occupying space resources. However, the insulation conditions for evaluating XLPE cable results using PDC techniques are often inaccurate because the parameters of the current Debye model OPEN ACCESS

results.title: An optical power supply scheme for ultrahigh voltage grid with high reliability
results.citation: Pu Wei et al. (2018) An optical power supply scheme for ultrahigh voltage grid with high reliability doi:10.1117/12.2502304
results.abstract: The optical power supply technology has been presented several years and used in Electric current transformer (ECT). However, the reliability of the technology is proved to be low in actual application, which limit its widely used in electric power industry. In this paper, we propose a novel optical power supply system for Ultra-High Voltage (UHV) Grid, which utilizes 810-nm laser diode in low-voltage (LV) environment and GaAs photocell for power supply in highvoltage (HV) environment. A Lithium battery is further employed in HV environment which makes the power supply scheme more flexible and reliable. In addition, two VCSELs are also used for information transmission between the LV and the HV environment. And the parameters of the system, such as load power, laser driving current, laser temperature, semiconductor chilling plate driving current, ambient temperature and ambient humidity, are collected, transmitted via optical fiber and processed by the power management scheme. The scheme is able to dynamically control the output power of the laser diode according to the load power variance and make the Lithium battery working when the optical power supply is unavailable or burst heavy power supply demand is emerged. In this way, the output power variance of the laser can be kept as low as possible, which effectively increase the reliability of the system.

results.title: A planning approach for AC/DC sending-end hybrid power grid with high-pentration renewable energy
results.citation: X. Zhang et al. (2020) A planning approach for AC/DC sending-end hybrid power grid with high-pentration renewable energy doi:10.1049/icp.2020.0364
results.venue: Workshop on Automated Control for Datacenters and Clouds
results.abstract: The sending-end power grid stability characteristics will be significantly affected after ultra-high voltage (UHV) DC projects operation and the large-scale renewable energy centralized connection. Based on the analysis of the interaction between the renewable energy, UHV DC project and the weak sending-end power grid, a practical planning approach for AC/DC hybrid power grid was proposed in this paper. The proposed approach, including the renewable energy power characteristics, UHV DC transmission scheme, UHV DC location selection, AC grid construction plan, typical operating mode, security and stability evaluation, clarified key issues in the power grid planning after UHV DC substation connected, which guaranteed the stable and reliable operation of the new energy unit, the UHV DC project and the sending-end power grid. This approach was applied to a real power grid, which was a new energy high-penetration sending-end power grid with a UHV DC project planned. The research results show that the approach proposed in this paper is very practical, because of that the analysis results can guide the construction of the second and the third defence systems related to DC project, and ensure the stability of the system frequency after serious fault of DC system.

results.title: DC Commutation Failure Analysis for the Sending-Side System Fault in Hybrid UHV Half-Wavelength and UHV DC Transmission System
results.citation: Qianyu Zhao et al. (2023) DC Commutation Failure Analysis for the Sending-Side System Fault in Hybrid UHV Half-Wavelength and UHV DC Transmission System doi:10.1080/15325008.2022.2163518
results.abstract: Abstract Commutation failure (CF) caused by the failure which occurs on the sending side of the ultra-high voltage (UHV) half-wavelength AC (HWAC) transmission system is studied. Firstly, a hybrid UHV HWAC and UHV DC transmission system model is built, and the essence of CF is revealed regarding the influences of various factors on occurrence probability of CF. Then, the mechanism of CF of the UHV DC transmission system caused by the fault at the HWAC line in the hybrid system is explored. The steady-state operation characteristics are obtained through characteristic analysis according to the quasi-steady-state model of the HWAC line, and a three-phase short-circuit fault is assumed on the sending-side of a HWAC line and its voltage characteristics are deeply analyzed. In the actual power system, the specific situation of DC CF caused by the fault of the HWAC line is verified and analyzed based on a large quantity of transient stability calculations. Comparative analysis of the effects on UHV DC lines of the faults which occur on different locations of a HWAC line is presented. The conclusions can provide a useful reference and reliable technical support for the development of HWAC system and maintain power grid security and stability.

results.title: Cross-Platform Electromagnetic Transient Real-Time Simulation Method of Large-Scale Power Grid
results.citation: Chongra Liu et al. (2023) Cross-Platform Electromagnetic Transient Real-Time Simulation Method of Large-Scale Power Grid doi:10.1109/EI259745.2023.10513065
results.venue: 2023 IEEE 7th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
results.abstract: With the advancement of Ultra-High Voltage (UHV) AC/DC hybrid power grids, the interactions between DC terminals have become increasingly apparent. In order to precisely simulate these interactions among regional power grids, innovative simulation methods are urgently needed. Building upon the foundation of existing digital-analog hybrid simulation technology, this research addresses the challenge of accommodating large-scale access and the actual system control system of protective devices. It achieves realtime, extensive grid electromagnetic transient simulation across multi-platforms. This simulation not only captures HVDC control protection features but also leverages cross-platform supercomputing technology to enhance simulation scalability. By overcoming the limitations of electromagnetic transient simulation scale, it addresses the challenge of high-precision simulation for UHV AC/DC large-scale hybrid grids. This paper explores the decoupling method for large-scale power grid cross-platform simulation and proposes a unified simulation data transmission and synchronization scheme. Utilizing this method, realtime simulations of interconnected power grids in the northwest and east China regions are conducted, significantly enhancing the overall realtime capabilities of electromagnetic transient simulations. A comparative analysis with actual power grid fault simulation validates the accuracy of cross-platform joint simulations. This study provides an effective simulation approach for in-depth analysis of interactions between sending and receiving ends in cross-regional power grids.

results.title: THE DISTRIBUTIONAL EFFECT OF INTER-REGIONAL TRANSMISSION GRID EXPANSION ON CHINA’S POWER INDUSTRY
results.citation: XU Tan et al. (2022) THE DISTRIBUTIONAL EFFECT OF INTER-REGIONAL TRANSMISSION GRID EXPANSION ON CHINA’S POWER INDUSTRY doi:10.1142/s201000782240005x
results.abstract: China suffers significant heterogeneities in resource endowments and geological conditions across the regions. Ultra-high voltage (UHV) line construction is a project put forward by the government to boost the inter-regional transmission grid expansion, benefiting economic development by reducing pollutant emissions and absorbing more clean energy. This study investigates the extent to which this giant infrastructure has distributional effects on China’s power industry. We estimate the distributional effect of UHV lines from the perspective of power deployment, carbon emissions, and producer surplus by simulating an equilibrium model of the national electricity market through the mixed complementarity problems (MCPs) method. Furthermore, the Lorenz curve is also employed to investigate the distributional effect of UHV introduction. The results indicate that: (1) the distributional effect of introducing UHV lines is regressive considering its limited contributions to the equalization of environmental and economic welfare; (2) the emission reduction effect of the UHV lines has been improved between 2015 and 2018; (3) power plants with a large installation capacity could seek more economic profits while emitting more pollution; (4) the deployment of clean power in the resource-abundant regions is far below its potential. Meanwhile, we propose improving the structure and technology of the power mix, which can accelerate China’s market-oriented reform of the power system by equally distributing the benefits.
